2272       (<symbol>InvalidTransactionId</symbol>) if the relation is not a table.
2273      </para></entry>
2274     </row>
2275
2276     <row>
2277      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2278       <structfield>relminmxid</structfield> <type>xid</type>
2279      </para>
2280      <para>
2281       All multixact IDs before this one have been replaced by a
2282       transaction ID in this table.  This is used to track
2283       whether the table needs to be vacuumed in order to prevent multixact ID
2284       wraparound or to allow <literal>pg_multixact</literal> to be shrunk.  Zero
2285       (<symbol>InvalidMultiXactId</symbol>) if the relation is not a table.
2286      </para></entry>
2287     </row>
2288
2289     <row>
2290      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2291       <structfield>relacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
2292      </para>
2293      <para>
2294       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
2295      </para></entry>
2296     </row>
2297
2298     <row>
2299      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2300       <structfield>reloptions</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
2301      </para>
2302      <para>
2303       Access-method-specific options, as <quote>keyword=value</quote> strings
2304      </para></entry>
2305     </row>
2306
2307     <row>
2308      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2309       <structfield>relpartbound</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
2310      </para>
2311      <para>
2312       If table is a partition (see <structfield>relispartition</structfield>),
2313       internal representation of the partition bound
2314      </para></entry>
2315     </row>
2316    </tbody>
2317   </tgroup>
2318  </table>
2319
2320  <para>
2321   Several of the Boolean flags in <structname>pg_class</structname> are maintained
2322   lazily: they are guaranteed to be true if that's the correct state, but
2323   may not be reset to false immediately when the condition is no longer
2324   true.  For example, <structfield>relhasindex</structfield> is set by
2325   <link linkend="sql-createindex"><command>CREATE INDEX</command></link>, but it is never cleared by
2326   <link linkend="sql-dropindex"><command>DROP INDEX</command></link>.  Instead, <link linkend="sql-vacuum"><command>VACUUM</command></link> clears
2327   <structfield>relhasindex</structfield> if it finds the table has no indexes.  This
2328   arrangement avoids race conditions and improves concurrency.
2329  </para>
2330 </sect1>
2331
2332 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-collation">
2333  <title><structname>pg_collation</structname></title>
2334
2335  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-collation">
2336   <primary>pg_collation</primary>
2337  </indexterm>
2338
2339  <para>
2340   The catalog <structname>pg_collation</structname> describes the
2341   available collations, which are essentially mappings from an SQL
2342   name to operating system locale categories.
2343   See <xref linkend="collation"/> for more information.
2344  </para>
2345
2346  <table>
2347   <title><structname>pg_collation</structname> Columns</title>
2348   <tgroup cols="1">
2349    <thead>
2350     <row>
2351      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2352       Column Type
2353      </para>
2354      <para>
2355       Description
2356      </para></entry>
2357     </row>
2358    </thead>
2359
2360    <tbody>
2361     <row>
2362      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2363       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2364      </para>
2365      <para>
2366       Row identifier
2367      </para></entry>
2368     </row>
2369
2370     <row>
2371      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2372       <structfield>collname</structfield> <type>name</type>
2373      </para>
2374      <para>
2375       Collation name (unique per namespace and encoding)
2376      </para></entry>
2377     </row>
2378
2379     <row>
2380      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2381       <structfield>collnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2382       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2383      </para>
2384      <para>
2385       The OID of the namespace that contains this collation
2386      </para></entry>
2387     </row>
2388
2389     <row>
2390      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2391       <structfield>collowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2392       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2393      </para>
2394      <para>
2395       Owner of the collation
2396      </para></entry>
2397     </row>
2398
2399     <row>
2400      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2401       <structfield>collprovider</structfield> <type>char</type>
2402      </para>
2403      <para>
2404       Provider of the collation: <literal>d</literal> = database default,
2405       <literal>b</literal> = builtin, <literal>c</literal> = libc,
2406       <literal>i</literal> = icu </para></entry>
2407     </row>
2408
2409     <row>
2410      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2411       <structfield>collisdeterministic</structfield> <type>bool</type>
2412      </para>
2413      <para>
2414       Is the collation deterministic?
2415      </para></entry>
2416     </row>
2417
2418     <row>
2419      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2420       <structfield>collencoding</structfield> <type>int4</type>
2421      </para>
2422      <para>
2423       Encoding in which the collation is applicable, or -1 if it
2424       works for any encoding
2425      </para></entry>
2426     </row>
2427
2428     <row>
2429      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2430       <structfield>collcollate</structfield> <type>text</type>
2431      </para>
2432      <para>
2433       <symbol>LC_COLLATE</symbol> for this collation object. If the provider is
2434       not <literal>libc</literal>, <structfield>collcollate</structfield> is
2435       <literal>NULL</literal> and <structfield>colllocale</structfield> is
2436       used instead.
2437      </para></entry>
2438     </row>
2439
2440     <row>
2441      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2442       <structfield>collctype</structfield> <type>text</type>
2443      </para>
2444      <para>
2445       <symbol>LC_CTYPE</symbol> for this collation object. If the provider is
2446       not <literal>libc</literal>, <structfield>collctype</structfield> is
2447       <literal>NULL</literal> and <structfield>colllocale</structfield> is
2448       used instead.
2449      </para></entry>
2450     </row>
2451
2452     <row>
2453      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2454       <structfield>colllocale</structfield> <type>text</type>
2455      </para>
2456      <para>
2457       Collation provider locale name for this collation object. If the
2458       provider is <literal>libc</literal>,
2459       <structfield>colllocale</structfield> is <literal>NULL</literal>;
2460       <structfield>collcollate</structfield> and
2461       <structfield>collctype</structfield> are used instead.
2462      </para></entry>
2463     </row>
2464
2465     <row>
2466      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2467       <structfield>collicurules</structfield> <type>text</type>
2468      </para>
2469      <para>
2470       ICU collation rules for this collation object
2471      </para></entry>
2472     </row>
2473
2474     <row>
2475      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2476       <structfield>collversion</structfield> <type>text</type>
2477      </para>
2478      <para>
2479       Provider-specific version of the collation.  This is recorded when the
2480       collation is created and then checked when it is used, to detect
2481       changes in the collation definition that could lead to data corruption.
2482      </para></entry>
2483     </row>
2484    </tbody>
2485   </tgroup>
2486  </table>
2487
2488  <para>
2489   Note that the unique key on this catalog is (<structfield>collname</structfield>,
2490   <structfield>collencoding</structfield>, <structfield>collnamespace</structfield>) not just
2491   (<structfield>collname</structfield>, <structfield>collnamespace</structfield>).
2492   <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> generally ignores all
2493   collations that do not have <structfield>collencoding</structfield> equal to
2494   either the current database's encoding or -1, and creation of new entries
2495   with the same name as an entry with <structfield>collencoding</structfield> = -1
2496   is forbidden.  Therefore it is sufficient to use a qualified SQL name
2497   (<replaceable>schema</replaceable>.<replaceable>name</replaceable>) to identify a collation,
2498   even though this is not unique according to the catalog definition.
2499   The reason for defining the catalog this way is that
2500   <application>initdb</application> fills it in at cluster initialization time with
2501   entries for all locales available on the system, so it must be able to
2502   hold entries for all encodings that might ever be used in the cluster.
2503  </para>
2504
2505  <para>
2506   In the <literal>template0</literal> database, it could be useful to create
2507   collations whose encoding does not match the database encoding,
2508   since they could match the encodings of databases later cloned from
2509   <literal>template0</literal>.  This would currently have to be done manually.
2510  </para>
2511 </sect1>
2512
2513 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-constraint">
2514  <title><structname>pg_constraint</structname></title>
2515
2516  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-constraint">
2517   <primary>pg_constraint</primary>
2518  </indexterm>
2519
2520  <para>
2521   The catalog <structname>pg_constraint</structname> stores check, not-null,
2522   primary key, unique, foreign key, and exclusion constraints on tables.
2523   (Column constraints are not treated specially.  Every column constraint is
2524   equivalent to some table constraint.)
2525  </para>
2526
2527  <para>
2528   User-defined constraint triggers (created with <link linkend="sql-createtrigger">
2529   <command>CREATE CONSTRAINT TRIGGER</command></link>) also give rise to an entry in this table.
2530  </para>
2531
2532  <para>
2533   Check constraints on domains are stored here, too.
2534  </para>
2535
2536  <table>
2537   <title><structname>pg_constraint</structname> Columns</title>
2538   <tgroup cols="1">
2539    <thead>
2540     <row>
2541      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2542       Column Type
2543      </para>
2544      <para>
2545       Description
2546      </para></entry>
2547     </row>
2548    </thead>
2549
2550    <tbody>
2551     <row>
2552      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2553       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2554      </para>
2555      <para>
2556       Row identifier
2557      </para></entry>
2558     </row>
2559
2560     <row>
2561      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2562       <structfield>conname</structfield> <type>name</type>
2563      </para>
2564      <para>
2565       Constraint name (not necessarily unique!)
2566      </para></entry>
2567     </row>
2568
2569     <row>
2570      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2571       <structfield>connamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2572       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2573      </para>
2574      <para>
2575       The OID of the namespace that contains this constraint
2576      </para></entry>
2577     </row>
2578
2579     <row>
2580      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2581       <structfield>contype</structfield> <type>char</type>
2582      </para>
2583      <para>
2584       <literal>c</literal> = check constraint,
2585       <literal>f</literal> = foreign key constraint,
2586       <literal>n</literal> = not-null constraint,
2587       <literal>p</literal> = primary key constraint,
2588       <literal>u</literal> = unique constraint,
2589       <literal>t</literal> = constraint trigger,
2590       <literal>x</literal> = exclusion constraint
2591      </para></entry>
2592     </row>
2593
2594     <row>
2595      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2596       <structfield>condeferrable</structfield> <type>bool</type>
2597      </para>
2598      <para>
2599       Is the constraint deferrable?
2600      </para></entry>
2601     </row>
2602
2603     <row>
2604      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2605       <structfield>condeferred</structfield> <type>bool</type>
2606      </para>
2607      <para>
2608       Is the constraint deferred by default?
2609      </para></entry>
2610     </row>
2611
2612     <row>
2613      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2614       <structfield>conenforced</structfield> <type>bool</type>
2615      </para>
2616      <para>
2617       Is the constraint enforced?
2618      </para></entry>
2619     </row>
2620
2621     <row>
2622      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2623       <structfield>convalidated</structfield> <type>bool</type>
2624      </para>
2625      <para>
2626       Has the constraint been validated?
2627      </para></entry>
2628     </row>
2629
2630     <row>
2631      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2632       <structfield>conrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2633       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2634      </para>
2635      <para>
2636       The table this constraint is on; zero if not a table constraint
2637      </para></entry>
2638     </row>
2639
2640     <row>
2641      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2642       <structfield>contypid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2643       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2644      </para>
2645      <para>
2646       The domain this constraint is on; zero if not a domain constraint
2647      </para></entry>
2648     </row>
2649
2650     <row>
2651      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2652       <structfield>conindid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2653       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2654      </para>
2655      <para>
2656       The index supporting this constraint, if it's a unique, primary
2657       key, foreign key, or exclusion constraint; else zero
2658      </para></entry>
2659     </row>
2660
2661     <row>
2662      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2663       <structfield>conparentid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2664       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-constraint"><structname>pg_constraint</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2665      </para>
2666      <para>
2667       The corresponding constraint of the parent partitioned table,
2668       if this is a constraint on a partition; else zero
2669      </para></entry>
2670     </row>
2671
2672     <row>
2673      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2674       <structfield>confrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2675       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2676      </para>
2677      <para>
2678       If a foreign key, the referenced table; else zero
2679      </para></entry>
2680     </row>
2681
2682     <row>
2683      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2684       <structfield>confupdtype</structfield> <type>char</type>
2685      </para>
2686      <para>
2687       Foreign key update action code:
2688       <literal>a</literal> = no action,
2689       <literal>r</literal> = restrict,
2690       <literal>c</literal> = cascade,
2691       <literal>n</literal> = set null,
2692       <literal>d</literal> = set default
2693      </para></entry>
2694     </row>
2695
2696     <row>
2697      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2698       <structfield>confdeltype</structfield> <type>char</type>
2699      </para>
2700      <para>
2701       Foreign key deletion action code:
2702       <literal>a</literal> = no action,
2703       <literal>r</literal> = restrict,
2704       <literal>c</literal> = cascade,
2705       <literal>n</literal> = set null,
2706       <literal>d</literal> = set default
2707      </para></entry>
2708     </row>
2709
2710     <row>
2711      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2712       <structfield>confmatchtype</structfield> <type>char</type>
2713      </para>
2714      <para>
2715       Foreign key match type:
2716       <literal>f</literal> = full,
2717       <literal>p</literal> = partial,
2718       <literal>s</literal> = simple
2719      </para></entry>
2720     </row>
2721
2722     <row>
2723      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2724       <structfield>conislocal</structfield> <type>bool</type>
2725      </para>
2726      <para>
2727       This constraint is defined locally for the relation.  Note that a
2728       constraint can be locally defined and inherited simultaneously.
2729      </para></entry>
2730     </row>
2731
2732     <row>
2733      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2734       <structfield>coninhcount</structfield> <type>int2</type>
2735      </para>
2736      <para>
2737       The number of direct inheritance ancestors this constraint has.
2738       A constraint with
2739       a nonzero number of ancestors cannot be dropped nor renamed.
2740      </para></entry>
2741     </row>
2742
2743     <row>
2744      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2745       <structfield>connoinherit</structfield> <type>bool</type>
2746      </para>
2747      <para>
2748       This constraint is defined locally for the relation.  It is a
2749       non-inheritable constraint.
2750      </para></entry>
2751     </row>
2752
2753     <row>
2754      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2755       <structfield>conperiod</structfield> <type>bool</type>
2756      </para>
2757      <para>
2758       This constraint is defined with <literal>WITHOUT OVERLAPS</literal>
2759       (for primary keys and unique constraints) or <literal>PERIOD</literal>
2760       (for foreign keys).
2761      </para></entry>
2762     </row>
2763
2764     <row>
2765      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2766       <structfield>conkey</structfield> <type>int2[]</type>
2767       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link>.<structfield>attnum</structfield>)
2768      </para>
2769      <para>
2770       If a table constraint (including foreign keys, but not constraint
2771       triggers), list of the constrained columns
2772      </para></entry>
2773     </row>
2774
2775     <row>
2776      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2777       <structfield>confkey</structfield> <type>int2[]</type>
2778       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link>.<structfield>attnum</structfield>)
2779      </para>
2780      <para>
2781       If a foreign key, list of the referenced columns
2782      </para></entry>
2783     </row>
2784
2785     <row>
2786      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2787       <structfield>conpfeqop</structfield> <type>oid[]</type>
2788       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-operator"><structname>pg_operator</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2789      </para>
2790      <para>
2791       If a foreign key, list of the equality operators for PK = FK comparisons
2792      </para></entry>
2793     </row>
2794
2795     <row>
2796      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2797       <structfield>conppeqop</structfield> <type>oid[]</type>
2798       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-operator"><structname>pg_operator</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2799      </para>
2800      <para>
2801       If a foreign key, list of the equality operators for PK = PK comparisons
2802      </para></entry>
2803     </row>
2804
2805     <row>
2806      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2807       <structfield>conffeqop</structfield> <type>oid[]</type>
2808       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-operator"><structname>pg_operator</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2809      </para>
2810      <para>
2811       If a foreign key, list of the equality operators for FK = FK comparisons
2812      </para></entry>
2813     </row>
2814
2815     <row>
2816      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2817       <structfield>confdelsetcols</structfield> <type>int2[]</type>
2818       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link>.<structfield>attnum</structfield>)
2819      </para>
2820      <para>
2821       If a foreign key with a <literal>SET NULL</literal> or <literal>SET
2822       DEFAULT</literal> delete action, the columns that will be updated.
2823       If null, all of the referencing columns will be updated.
2824      </para></entry>
2825     </row>
2826
2827     <row>
2828      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2829       <structfield>conexclop</structfield> <type>oid[]</type>
2830       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-operator"><structname>pg_operator</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2831      </para>
2832      <para>
2833       If an exclusion constraint or <literal>WITHOUT OVERLAPS</literal>
2834       primary key/unique constraint, list of the per-column exclusion operators.
2835      </para></entry>
2836     </row>
2837
2838     <row>
2839      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2840       <structfield>conbin</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
2841      </para>
2842      <para>
2843       If a check constraint, an internal representation of the
2844       expression.  (It's recommended to use
2845       <function>pg_get_constraintdef()</function> to extract the definition of
2846       a check constraint.)
2847      </para></entry>
2848     </row>
2849    </tbody>
2850   </tgroup>
2851  </table>
2852
2853  <para>
2854   In the case of an exclusion constraint, <structfield>conkey</structfield>
2855   is only useful for constraint elements that are simple column references.
2856   For other cases, a zero appears in <structfield>conkey</structfield>
2857   and the associated index must be consulted to discover the expression
2858   that is constrained.  (<structfield>conkey</structfield> thus has the
2859   same contents as <link linkend="catalog-pg-index"><structname>pg_index</structname></link>.<structfield>indkey</structfield> for the
2860   index.)
2861  </para>
2862
2863  <note>
2864   <para>
2865    <literal>pg_class.relchecks</literal> needs to agree with the
2866    number of check-constraint entries found in this table for each
2867    relation.
2868   </para>
2869  </note>
2870 </sect1>
2871
2872
2873 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-conversion">
2874  <title><structname>pg_conversion</structname></title>
2875
2876  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-conversion">
2877   <primary>pg_conversion</primary>
2878  </indexterm>
2879
2880  <para>
2881   The catalog <structname>pg_conversion</structname> describes
2882   encoding conversion functions.  See <xref linkend="sql-createconversion"/>
2883   for more information.
2884  </para>
2885
2886  <table>
2887   <title><structname>pg_conversion</structname> Columns</title>
2888   <tgroup cols="1">
2889    <thead>
2890     <row>
2891      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2892       Column Type
2893      </para>
2894      <para>
2895       Description
2896      </para></entry>
2897     </row>
2898    </thead>
2899
2900    <tbody>
2901     <row>
2902      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2903       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2904      </para>
2905      <para>
2906       Row identifier
2907      </para></entry>
2908     </row>
2909
2910     <row>
2911      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2912       <structfield>conname</structfield> <type>name</type>
2913      </para>
2914      <para>
2915       Conversion name (unique within a namespace)
2916      </para></entry>
2917     </row>
2918
2919     <row>
2920      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2921       <structfield>connamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2922       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2923      </para>
2924      <para>
2925       The OID of the namespace that contains this conversion
2926      </para></entry>
2927     </row>
2928
2929     <row>
2930      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2931       <structfield>conowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
2932       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2933      </para>
2934      <para>
2935       Owner of the conversion
2936      </para></entry>
2937     </row>
2938
2939     <row>
2940      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2941       <structfield>conforencoding</structfield> <type>int4</type>
2942      </para>
2943      <para>
2944       Source encoding ID (<link linkend="pg-encoding-to-char"><function>pg_encoding_to_char()</function></link>
2945       can translate this number to the encoding name)
2946      </para></entry>
2947     </row>
2948
2949     <row>
2950      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2951       <structfield>contoencoding</structfield> <type>int4</type>
2952      </para>
2953      <para>
2954       Destination encoding ID (<link linkend="pg-encoding-to-char"><function>pg_encoding_to_char()</function></link>
2955       can translate this number to the encoding name)
2956      </para></entry>
2957     </row>
2958
2959     <row>
2960      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2961       <structfield>conproc</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
2962       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
2963      </para>
2964      <para>
2965       Conversion function
2966      </para></entry>
2967     </row>
2968
2969     <row>
2970      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
2971       <structfield>condefault</structfield> <type>bool</type>
2972      </para>
2973      <para>
2974       True if this is the default conversion
2975      </para></entry>
2976     </row>
2977    </tbody>
2978   </tgroup>
2979  </table>
2980
2981 </sect1>
2982
2983 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-database">
2984  <title><structname>pg_database</structname></title>
2985
2986  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-database">
2987   <primary>pg_database</primary>
2988  </indexterm>
2989
2990  <para>
2991   The catalog <structname>pg_database</structname> stores information about
2992   the available databases.  Databases are created with the <link
2993   linkend="sql-createdatabase"><command>CREATE DATABASE</command></link> command.
2994   Consult <xref linkend="managing-databases"/> for details about the meaning
2995   of some of the parameters.
2996  </para>
2997
2998  <para>
2999   Unlike most system catalogs, <structname>pg_database</structname>
3000   is shared across all databases of a cluster: there is only one
3001   copy of <structname>pg_database</structname> per cluster, not
3002   one per database.
3003  </para>
3004
3005  <table>
3006   <title><structname>pg_database</structname> Columns</title>
3007   <tgroup cols="1">
3008    <thead>
3009     <row>
3010      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3011       Column Type
3012      </para>
3013      <para>
3014       Description
3015      </para></entry>
3016     </row>
3017    </thead>
3018
3019    <tbody>
3020     <row>
3021      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3022       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3023      </para>
3024      <para>
3025       Row identifier
3026      </para></entry>
3027     </row>
3028
3029     <row>
3030      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3031       <structfield>datname</structfield> <type>name</type>
3032      </para>
3033      <para>
3034       Database name
3035      </para></entry>
3036     </row>
3037
3038     <row>
3039      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3040       <structfield>datdba</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3041       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3042      </para>
3043      <para>
3044       Owner of the database, usually the user who created it
3045      </para></entry>
3046     </row>
3047
3048     <row>
3049      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3050       <structfield>encoding</structfield> <type>int4</type>
3051      </para>
3052      <para>
3053       Character encoding for this database
3054       (<link linkend="pg-encoding-to-char"><function>pg_encoding_to_char()</function></link> can translate
3055       this number to the encoding name)
3056      </para></entry>
3057     </row>
3058
3059     <row>
3060      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3061       <structfield>datlocprovider</structfield> <type>char</type>
3062      </para>
3063      <para>
3064       Locale provider for this database: <literal>b</literal> = builtin,
3065       <literal>c</literal> = libc, <literal>i</literal> = icu </para></entry>
3066     </row>
3067
3068     <row>
3069      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3070       <structfield>datistemplate</structfield> <type>bool</type>
3071      </para>
3072      <para>
3073       If true, then this database can be cloned by
3074       any user with <literal>CREATEDB</literal> privileges;
3075       if false, then only superusers or the owner of
3076       the database can clone it.
3077      </para></entry>
3078     </row>
3079
3080     <row>
3081      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3082       <structfield>datallowconn</structfield> <type>bool</type>
3083      </para>
3084      <para>
3085       If false then no one can connect to this database.  This is
3086       used to protect the <literal>template0</literal> database from being altered.
3087      </para></entry>
3088     </row>
3089
3090     <row>
3091      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3092       <structfield>dathasloginevt</structfield> <type>bool</type>
3093      </para>
3094      <para>
3095        Indicates that there are login event triggers defined for this database.
3096        This flag is used to avoid extra lookups on the
3097        <structname>pg_event_trigger</structname> table during each backend
3098        startup.  This flag is used internally by <productname>PostgreSQL</productname>
3099        and should not be manually altered or read for monitoring purposes.
3100      </para></entry>
3101     </row>
3102
3103     <row>
3104      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3105       <structfield>datconnlimit</structfield> <type>int4</type>
3106      </para>
3107      <para>
3108       Sets maximum number of concurrent connections that can be made
3109       to this database.  -1 means no limit, -2 indicates the database is
3110       invalid.
3111      </para></entry>
3112     </row>
3113
3114     <row>
3115      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3116       <structfield>datfrozenxid</structfield> <type>xid</type>
3117      </para>
3118      <para>
3119       All transaction IDs before this one have been replaced with a permanent
3120       (<quote>frozen</quote>) transaction ID in this database.  This is used to
3121       track whether the database needs to be vacuumed in order to prevent
3122       transaction ID wraparound or to allow <literal>pg_xact</literal> to be shrunk.
3123       It is the minimum of the per-table
3124       <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>relfrozenxid</structfield> values.
3125      </para></entry>
3126     </row>
3127
3128     <row>
3129      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3130       <structfield>datminmxid</structfield> <type>xid</type>
3131      </para>
3132      <para>
3133       All multixact IDs before this one have been replaced with a
3134       transaction ID in this database.  This is used to
3135       track whether the database needs to be vacuumed in order to prevent
3136       multixact ID wraparound or to allow <literal>pg_multixact</literal> to be shrunk.
3137       It is the minimum of the per-table
3138       <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>relminmxid</structfield> values.
3139      </para></entry>
3140     </row>
3141
3142     <row>
3143      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3144       <structfield>dattablespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3145       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-tablespace"><structname>pg_tablespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3146      </para>
3147      <para>
3148       The default tablespace for the database.
3149       Within this database, all tables for which
3150       <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>reltablespace</structfield> is zero
3151       will be stored in this tablespace; in particular, all the non-shared
3152       system catalogs will be there.
3153      </para></entry>
3154     </row>
3155
3156     <row>
3157      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3158       <structfield>datcollate</structfield> <type>text</type>
3159      </para>
3160      <para>
3161       LC_COLLATE for this database
3162      </para></entry>
3163     </row>
3164
3165     <row>
3166      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3167       <structfield>datctype</structfield> <type>text</type>
3168      </para>
3169      <para>
3170       LC_CTYPE for this database
3171      </para></entry>
3172     </row>
3173
3174     <row>
3175      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3176       <structfield>datlocale</structfield> <type>text</type>
3177      </para>
3178      <para>
3179       Collation provider locale name for this database. If the
3180       provider is <literal>libc</literal>,
3181       <structfield>datlocale</structfield> is <literal>NULL</literal>;
3182       <structfield>datcollate</structfield> and
3183       <structfield>datctype</structfield> are used instead.
3184      </para></entry>
3185     </row>
3186
3187     <row>
3188      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3189       <structfield>daticurules</structfield> <type>text</type>
3190      </para>
3191      <para>
3192       ICU collation rules for this database
3193      </para></entry>
3194     </row>
3195
3196     <row>
3197      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3198       <structfield>datcollversion</structfield> <type>text</type>
3199      </para>
3200      <para>
3201       Provider-specific version of the collation.  This is recorded when the
3202       database is created and then checked when it is used, to detect
3203       changes in the collation definition that could lead to data corruption.
3204      </para></entry>
3205     </row>
3206
3207     <row>
3208      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3209       <structfield>datacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
3210      </para>
3211      <para>
3212       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
3213      </para></entry>
3214     </row>
3215    </tbody>
3216   </tgroup>
3217  </table>
3218 </sect1>
3219
3220
3221 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-db-role-setting">
3222  <title><structname>pg_db_role_setting</structname></title>
3223
3224  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-db-role-setting">
3225   <primary>pg_db_role_setting</primary>
3226  </indexterm>
3227
3228  <para>
3229   The catalog <structname>pg_db_role_setting</structname> records the default
3230   values that have been set for run-time configuration variables,
3231   for each role and database combination.
3232  </para>
3233
3234  <para>
3235   Unlike most system catalogs, <structname>pg_db_role_setting</structname>
3236   is shared across all databases of a cluster: there is only one
3237   copy of <structname>pg_db_role_setting</structname> per cluster, not
3238   one per database.
3239  </para>
3240
3241  <table>
3242   <title><structname>pg_db_role_setting</structname> Columns</title>
3243   <tgroup cols="1">
3244    <thead>
3245     <row>
3246      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3247       Column Type
3248      </para>
3249      <para>
3250       Description
3251      </para></entry>
3252     </row>
3253    </thead>
3254
3255    <tbody>
3256     <row>
3257      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3258       <structfield>setdatabase</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3259       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-database"><structname>pg_database</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3260      </para>
3261      <para>
3262       The OID of the database the setting is applicable to, or zero if not database-specific
3263      </para></entry>
3264     </row>
3265
3266     <row>
3267      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3268       <structfield>setrole</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3269       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3270      </para>
3271      <para>
3272       The OID of the role the setting is applicable to, or zero if not role-specific
3273      </para></entry>
3274     </row>
3275
3276     <row>
3277      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3278       <structfield>setconfig</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
3279      </para>
3280      <para>
3281       Defaults for run-time configuration variables
3282      </para></entry>
3283     </row>
3284    </tbody>
3285   </tgroup>
3286  </table>
3287 </sect1>
3288
3289
3290 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-default-acl">
3291  <title><structname>pg_default_acl</structname></title>
3292
3293  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-default-acl">
3294   <primary>pg_default_acl</primary>
3295  </indexterm>
3296
3297  <para>
3298   The catalog <structname>pg_default_acl</structname> stores initial
3299   privileges to be assigned to newly created objects.
3300  </para>
3301
3302  <table>
3303   <title><structname>pg_default_acl</structname> Columns</title>
3304   <tgroup cols="1">
3305    <thead>
3306     <row>
3307      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3308       Column Type
3309      </para>
3310      <para>
3311       Description
3312      </para></entry>
3313     </row>
3314    </thead>
3315
3316    <tbody>
3317     <row>
3318      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3319       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3320      </para>
3321      <para>
3322       Row identifier
3323      </para></entry>
3324     </row>
3325
3326     <row>
3327      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3328       <structfield>defaclrole</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3329       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3330      </para>
3331      <para>
3332       The OID of the role associated with this entry
3333      </para></entry>
3334     </row>
3335
3336     <row>
3337      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3338       <structfield>defaclnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3339       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3340      </para>
3341      <para>
3342       The OID of the namespace associated with this entry,
3343       or zero if none
3344      </para></entry>
3345     </row>
3346
3347     <row>
3348      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3349       <structfield>defaclobjtype</structfield> <type>char</type>
3350      </para>
3351      <para>
3352       Type of object this entry is for:
3353       <literal>r</literal> = relation (table, view),
3354       <literal>S</literal> = sequence,
3355       <literal>f</literal> = function,
3356       <literal>T</literal> = type,
3357       <literal>n</literal> = schema,
3358       <literal>L</literal> = large object
3359      </para></entry>
3360     </row>
3361
3362     <row>
3363      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3364       <structfield>defaclacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
3365      </para>
3366      <para>
3367       Access privileges that this type of object should have on creation
3368      </para></entry>
3369     </row>
3370    </tbody>
3371   </tgroup>
3372  </table>
3373
3374  <para>
3375   A <structname>pg_default_acl</structname> entry shows the initial privileges to
3376   be assigned to an object belonging to the indicated user.  There are
3377   currently two types of entry: <quote>global</quote> entries with
3378   <structfield>defaclnamespace</structfield> = zero, and <quote>per-schema</quote> entries
3379   that reference a particular schema.  If a global entry is present then
3380   it <emphasis>overrides</emphasis> the normal hard-wired default privileges
3381   for the object type.  A per-schema entry, if present, represents privileges
3382   to be <emphasis>added to</emphasis> the global or hard-wired default privileges.
3383  </para>
3384
3385  <para>
3386   Note that when an <acronym>ACL</acronym> entry in another catalog is null,
3387   it is taken to represent the hard-wired default privileges for its object,
3388   <emphasis>not</emphasis> whatever might be in <structname>pg_default_acl</structname>
3389   at the moment.  <structname>pg_default_acl</structname> is only consulted during
3390   object creation.
3391  </para>
3392
3393 </sect1>
3394
3395
3396 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-depend">
3397  <title><structname>pg_depend</structname></title>
3398
3399  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-depend">
3400   <primary>pg_depend</primary>
3401  </indexterm>
3402
3403  <para>
3404   The catalog <structname>pg_depend</structname> records the dependency
3405   relationships between database objects.  This information allows
3406   <command>DROP</command> commands to find which other objects must be dropped
3407   by <command>DROP CASCADE</command> or prevent dropping in the <command>DROP
3408   RESTRICT</command> case.
3409  </para>
3410
3411  <para>
3412   See also <link linkend="catalog-pg-shdepend"><structname>pg_shdepend</structname></link>,
3413   which performs a similar function for dependencies involving objects
3414   that are shared across a database cluster.
3415  </para>
3416
3417  <table>
3418   <title><structname>pg_depend</structname> Columns</title>
3419   <tgroup cols="1">
3420    <thead>
3421     <row>
3422      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3423       Column Type
3424      </para>
3425      <para>
3426       Description
3427      </para></entry>
3428     </row>
3429    </thead>
3430
3431    <tbody>
3432     <row>
3433      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3434       <structfield>classid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3435       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3436      </para>
3437      <para>
3438       The OID of the system catalog the dependent object is in
3439      </para></entry>
3440     </row>
3441
3442     <row>
3443      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3444       <structfield>objid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3445       (references any OID column)
3446      </para>
3447      <para>
3448       The OID of the specific dependent object
3449      </para></entry>
3450     </row>
3451
3452     <row>
3453      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3454       <structfield>objsubid</structfield> <type>int4</type>
3455      </para>
3456      <para>
3457       For a table column, this is the column number (the
3458       <structfield>objid</structfield> and <structfield>classid</structfield> refer to the
3459       table itself).  For all other object types, this column is
3460       zero.
3461      </para></entry>
3462     </row>
3463
3464     <row>
3465      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3466       <structfield>refclassid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3467       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3468      </para>
3469      <para>
3470       The OID of the system catalog the referenced object is in
3471      </para></entry>
3472     </row>
3473
3474     <row>
3475      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3476       <structfield>refobjid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3477       (references any OID column)
3478      </para>
3479      <para>
3480       The OID of the specific referenced object
3481      </para></entry>
3482     </row>
3483
3484     <row>
3485      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3486       <structfield>refobjsubid</structfield> <type>int4</type>
3487      </para>
3488      <para>
3489       For a table column, this is the column number (the
3490       <structfield>refobjid</structfield> and <structfield>refclassid</structfield> refer
3491       to the table itself).  For all other object types, this column
3492       is zero.
3493      </para></entry>
3494     </row>
3495
3496     <row>
3497      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3498       <structfield>deptype</structfield> <type>char</type>
3499      </para>
3500      <para>
3501       A code defining the specific semantics of this dependency relationship; see text
3502      </para></entry>
3503     </row>
3504    </tbody>
3505   </tgroup>
3506  </table>
3507
3508  <para>
3509   In all cases, a <structname>pg_depend</structname> entry indicates that the
3510   referenced object cannot be dropped without also dropping the dependent
3511   object.  However, there are several subflavors identified by
3512   <structfield>deptype</structfield>:
3513
3514   <variablelist>
3515    <varlistentry>
3516     <term><symbol>DEPENDENCY_NORMAL</symbol> (<literal>n</literal>)</term>
3517     <listitem>
3518      <para>
3519       A normal relationship between separately-created objects.  The
3520       dependent object can be dropped without affecting the
3521       referenced object.  The referenced object can only be dropped
3522       by specifying <literal>CASCADE</literal>, in which case the dependent
3523       object is dropped, too.  Example: a table column has a normal
3524       dependency on its data type.
3525      </para>
3526     </listitem>
3527    </varlistentry>
3528
3529    <varlistentry>
3530     <term><symbol>DEPENDENCY_AUTO</symbol> (<literal>a</literal>)</term>
3531     <listitem>
3532      <para>
3533       The dependent object can be dropped separately from the
3534       referenced object, and should be automatically dropped
3535       (regardless of <literal>RESTRICT</literal> or <literal>CASCADE</literal>
3536       mode) if the referenced object is dropped.  Example: a named
3537       constraint on a table is made auto-dependent on the table, so
3538       that it will go away if the table is dropped.
3539      </para>
3540     </listitem>
3541    </varlistentry>
3542
3543    <varlistentry>
3544     <term><symbol>DEPENDENCY_INTERNAL</symbol> (<literal>i</literal>)</term>
3545     <listitem>
3546      <para>
3547       The dependent object was created as part of creation of the
3548       referenced object, and is really just a part of its internal
3549       implementation.  A direct <command>DROP</command> of the dependent
3550       object will be disallowed outright (we'll tell the user to issue
3551       a <command>DROP</command> against the referenced object, instead).
3552       A <command>DROP</command> of the referenced object will result in
3553       automatically dropping the dependent object
3554       whether <literal>CASCADE</literal> is specified or not.  If the
3555       dependent object has to be dropped due to a dependency on some other
3556       object being removed, its drop is converted to a drop of the referenced
3557       object, so that <literal>NORMAL</literal> and <literal>AUTO</literal>
3558       dependencies of the dependent object behave much like they were
3559       dependencies of the referenced object.
3560       Example: a view's <literal>ON SELECT</literal> rule is made
3561       internally dependent on the view, preventing it from being dropped
3562       while the view remains.  Dependencies of the rule (such as tables it
3563       refers to) act as if they were dependencies of the view.
3564      </para>
3565     </listitem>
3566    </varlistentry>
3567
3568    <varlistentry>
3569     <term><symbol>DEPENDENCY_PARTITION_PRI</symbol> (<literal>P</literal>)</term>
3570     <term><symbol>DEPENDENCY_PARTITION_SEC</symbol> (<literal>S</literal>)</term>
3571     <listitem>
3572      <para>
3573       The dependent object was created as part of creation of the
3574       referenced object, and is really just a part of its internal
3575       implementation; however, unlike <literal>INTERNAL</literal>,
3576       there is more than one such referenced object.  The dependent object
3577       must not be dropped unless at least one of these referenced objects
3578       is dropped; if any one is, the dependent object should be dropped
3579       whether or not <literal>CASCADE</literal> is specified.  Also
3580       unlike <literal>INTERNAL</literal>, a drop of some other object
3581       that the dependent object depends on does not result in automatic
3582       deletion of any partition-referenced object.  Hence, if the drop
3583       does not cascade to at least one of these objects via some other
3584       path, it will be refused.  (In most cases, the dependent object
3585       shares all its non-partition dependencies with at least one
3586       partition-referenced object, so that this restriction does not
3587       result in blocking any cascaded delete.)
3588       Primary and secondary partition dependencies behave identically
3589       except that the primary dependency is preferred for use in error
3590       messages; hence, a partition-dependent object should have one
3591       primary partition dependency and one or more secondary partition
3592       dependencies.
3593       Note that partition dependencies are made in addition to, not
3594       instead of, any dependencies the object would normally have.  This
3595       simplifies <command>ATTACH/DETACH PARTITION</command> operations:
3596       the partition dependencies need only be added or removed.
3597       Example: a child partitioned index is made partition-dependent
3598       on both the partition table it is on and the parent partitioned
3599       index, so that it goes away if either of those is dropped, but
3600       not otherwise.  The dependency on the parent index is primary,
3601       so that if the user tries to drop the child partitioned index,
3602       the error message will suggest dropping the parent index instead
3603       (not the table).
3604      </para>
3605     </listitem>
3606    </varlistentry>
3607
3608    <varlistentry>
3609     <term><symbol>DEPENDENCY_EXTENSION</symbol> (<literal>e</literal>)</term>
3610     <listitem>
3611      <para>
3612       The dependent object is a member of the <firstterm>extension</firstterm> that is
3613       the referenced object (see
3614       <link linkend="catalog-pg-extension"><structname>pg_extension</structname></link>).
3615       The dependent object can be dropped only via
3616       <link linkend="sql-dropextension"><command>DROP EXTENSION</command></link> on the referenced object.
3617       Functionally this dependency type acts the same as
3618       an <literal>INTERNAL</literal> dependency, but it's kept separate for
3619       clarity and to simplify <application>pg_dump</application>.
3620      </para>
3621     </listitem>
3622    </varlistentry>
3623
3624    <varlistentry>
3625     <term><symbol>DEPENDENCY_AUTO_EXTENSION</symbol> (<literal>x</literal>)</term>
3626     <listitem>
3627      <para>
3628       The dependent object is not a member of the extension that is the
3629       referenced object (and so it should not be ignored
3630       by <application>pg_dump</application>), but it cannot function
3631       without the extension and should be auto-dropped if the extension is.
3632       The dependent object may be dropped on its own as well.
3633       Functionally this dependency type acts the same as
3634       an <literal>AUTO</literal> dependency, but it's kept separate for
3635       clarity and to simplify <application>pg_dump</application>.
3636      </para>
3637     </listitem>
3638    </varlistentry>
3639   </variablelist>
3640
3641   Other dependency flavors might be needed in future.
3642  </para>
3643
3644  <para>
3645   Note that it's quite possible for two objects to be linked by more than
3646   one <structname>pg_depend</structname> entry.  For example, a child
3647   partitioned index would have both a partition-type dependency on its
3648   associated partition table, and an auto dependency on each column of
3649   that table that it indexes.  This sort of situation expresses the union
3650   of multiple dependency semantics.  A dependent object can be dropped
3651   without <literal>CASCADE</literal> if any of its dependencies satisfies
3652   its condition for automatic dropping.  Conversely, all the
3653   dependencies' restrictions about which objects must be dropped together
3654   must be satisfied.
3655  </para>
3656
3657  <para>
3658   Most objects created during <application>initdb</application> are
3659   considered <quote>pinned</quote>, which means that the system itself
3660   depends on them.  Therefore, they are never allowed to be dropped.
3661   Also, knowing that pinned objects will not be dropped, the dependency
3662   mechanism doesn't bother to make <structname>pg_depend</structname>
3663   entries showing dependencies on them.  Thus, for example, a table
3664   column of type <type>numeric</type> notionally has
3665   a <literal>NORMAL</literal> dependency on the <type>numeric</type>
3666   data type, but no such entry actually appears
3667   in <structname>pg_depend</structname>.
3668  </para>
3669
3670 </sect1>
3671
3672
3673 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-description">
3674  <title><structname>pg_description</structname></title>
3675
3676  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-description">
3677   <primary>pg_description</primary>
3678  </indexterm>
3679
3680  <para>
3681   The catalog <structname>pg_description</structname> stores optional descriptions
3682   (comments) for each database object.  Descriptions can be manipulated
3683   with the <link linkend="sql-comment"><command>COMMENT</command></link> command and viewed with
3684   <application>psql</application>'s <literal>\d</literal> commands.
3685   Descriptions of many built-in system objects are provided in the initial
3686   contents of <structname>pg_description</structname>.
3687  </para>
3688
3689  <para>
3690   See also <link linkend="catalog-pg-shdescription"><structname>pg_shdescription</structname></link>,
3691   which performs a similar function for descriptions involving objects that
3692   are shared across a database cluster.
3693  </para>
3694
3695  <table>
3696   <title><structname>pg_description</structname> Columns</title>
3697   <tgroup cols="1">
3698    <thead>
3699     <row>
3700      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3701       Column Type
3702      </para>
3703      <para>
3704       Description
3705      </para></entry>
3706     </row>
3707    </thead>
3708
3709    <tbody>
3710     <row>
3711      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3712       <structfield>objoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3713       (references any OID column)
3714      </para>
3715      <para>
3716       The OID of the object this description pertains to
3717      </para></entry>
3718     </row>
3719
3720     <row>
3721      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3722       <structfield>classoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3723       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3724      </para>
3725      <para>
3726       The OID of the system catalog this object appears in
3727      </para></entry>
3728     </row>
3729
3730     <row>
3731      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3732       <structfield>objsubid</structfield> <type>int4</type>
3733      </para>
3734      <para>
3735       For a comment on a table column, this is the column number (the
3736       <structfield>objoid</structfield> and <structfield>classoid</structfield> refer to
3737       the table itself).  For all other object types, this column is
3738       zero.
3739      </para></entry>
3740     </row>
3741
3742     <row>
3743      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3744       <structfield>description</structfield> <type>text</type>
3745      </para>
3746      <para>
3747       Arbitrary text that serves as the description of this object
3748      </para></entry>
3749     </row>
3750    </tbody>
3751   </tgroup>
3752  </table>
3753
3754 </sect1>
3755
3756
3757 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-enum">
3758  <title><structname>pg_enum</structname></title>
3759
3760  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-enum">
3761   <primary>pg_enum</primary>
3762  </indexterm>
3763
3764  <para>
3765   The <structname>pg_enum</structname> catalog contains entries
3766   showing the values and labels for each enum type. The
3767   internal representation of a given enum value is actually the OID
3768   of its associated row in <structname>pg_enum</structname>.
3769  </para>
3770
3771  <table>
3772   <title><structname>pg_enum</structname> Columns</title>
3773   <tgroup cols="1">
3774    <thead>
3775     <row>
3776      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3777       Column Type
3778      </para>
3779      <para>
3780       Description
3781      </para></entry>
3782     </row>
3783    </thead>
3784
3785    <tbody>
3786     <row>
3787      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3788       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3789      </para>
3790      <para>
3791       Row identifier
3792      </para></entry>
3793     </row>
3794
3795     <row>
3796      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3797       <structfield>enumtypid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3798       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3799      </para>
3800      <para>
3801       The OID of the <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link> entry owning this enum value
3802      </para></entry>
3803     </row>
3804
3805     <row>
3806      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3807       <structfield>enumsortorder</structfield> <type>float4</type>
3808      </para>
3809      <para>
3810       The sort position of this enum value within its enum type
3811      </para></entry>
3812     </row>
3813
3814     <row>
3815      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3816       <structfield>enumlabel</structfield> <type>name</type>
3817      </para>
3818      <para>
3819       The textual label for this enum value
3820      </para></entry>
3821     </row>
3822    </tbody>
3823   </tgroup>
3824  </table>
3825
3826  <para>
3827   The OIDs for <structname>pg_enum</structname> rows follow a special
3828   rule: even-numbered OIDs are guaranteed to be ordered in the same way
3829   as the sort ordering of their enum type.  That is, if two even OIDs
3830   belong to the same enum type, the smaller OID must have the smaller
3831   <structfield>enumsortorder</structfield> value.  Odd-numbered OID values
3832   need bear no relationship to the sort order.  This rule allows the
3833   enum comparison routines to avoid catalog lookups in many common cases.
3834   The routines that create and alter enum types attempt to assign even
3835   OIDs to enum values whenever possible.
3836  </para>
3837
3838  <para>
3839   When an enum type is created, its members are assigned sort-order
3840   positions 1..<replaceable>n</replaceable>.  But members added later might be given
3841   negative or fractional values of <structfield>enumsortorder</structfield>.
3842   The only requirement on these values is that they be correctly
3843   ordered and unique within each enum type.
3844  </para>
3845 </sect1>
3846
3847
3848 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-event-trigger">
3849  <title><structname>pg_event_trigger</structname></title>
3850
3851  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-event-trigger">
3852   <primary>pg_event_trigger</primary>
3853  </indexterm>
3854
3855  <para>
3856   The catalog <structname>pg_event_trigger</structname> stores event triggers.
3857   See <xref linkend="event-triggers"/> for more information.
3858  </para>
3859
3860  <table>
3861   <title><structname>pg_event_trigger</structname> Columns</title>
3862   <tgroup cols="1">
3863    <thead>
3864     <row>
3865      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3866       Column Type
3867      </para>
3868      <para>
3869       Description
3870      </para></entry>
3871     </row>
3872    </thead>
3873
3874    <tbody>
3875     <row>
3876      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3877       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3878      </para>
3879      <para>
3880       Row identifier
3881      </para></entry>
3882     </row>
3883
3884     <row>
3885      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3886       <structfield>evtname</structfield> <type>name</type>
3887      </para>
3888      <para>
3889       Trigger name (must be unique)
3890      </para></entry>
3891     </row>
3892
3893     <row>
3894      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3895       <structfield>evtevent</structfield> <type>name</type>
3896      </para>
3897      <para>
3898       Identifies the event for which this trigger fires
3899      </para></entry>
3900     </row>
3901
3902     <row>
3903      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3904       <structfield>evtowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3905       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3906      </para>
3907      <para>
3908       Owner of the event trigger
3909      </para></entry>
3910     </row>
3911
3912     <row>
3913      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3914       <structfield>evtfoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3915       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
3916      </para>
3917      <para>
3918       The function to be called
3919      </para></entry>
3920     </row>
3921
3922     <row>
3923      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3924       <structfield>evtenabled</structfield> <type>char</type>
3925      </para>
3926      <para>
3927       Controls in which <xref linkend="guc-session-replication-role"/> modes
3928       the event trigger fires.
3929       <literal>O</literal> = trigger fires in <quote>origin</quote> and <quote>local</quote> modes,
3930       <literal>D</literal> = trigger is disabled,
3931       <literal>R</literal> = trigger fires in <quote>replica</quote> mode,
3932       <literal>A</literal> = trigger fires always.
3933      </para></entry>
3934     </row>
3935
3936     <row>
3937      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3938       <structfield>evttags</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
3939      </para>
3940      <para>
3941       Command tags for which this trigger will fire.  If NULL, the firing
3942       of this trigger is not restricted on the basis of the command tag.
3943      </para></entry>
3944     </row>
3945    </tbody>
3946   </tgroup>
3947  </table>
3948 </sect1>
3949
3950
3951 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-extension">
3952  <title><structname>pg_extension</structname></title>
3953
3954  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-extension">
3955   <primary>pg_extension</primary>
3956  </indexterm>
3957
3958  <para>
3959   The catalog <structname>pg_extension</structname> stores information
3960   about the installed extensions.  See <xref linkend="extend-extensions"/>
3961   for details about extensions.
3962  </para>
3963
3964  <table>
3965   <title><structname>pg_extension</structname> Columns</title>
3966   <tgroup cols="1">
3967    <thead>
3968     <row>
3969      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3970       Column Type
3971      </para>
3972      <para>
3973       Description
3974      </para></entry>
3975     </row>
3976    </thead>
3977
3978    <tbody>
3979     <row>
3980      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3981       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
3982      </para>
3983      <para>
3984       Row identifier
3985      </para></entry>
3986     </row>
3987
3988     <row>
3989      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3990       <structfield>extname</structfield> <type>name</type>
3991      </para>
3992      <para>
3993       Name of the extension
3994      </para></entry>
3995     </row>
3996
3997     <row>
3998      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
3999       <structfield>extowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4000       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4001      </para>
4002      <para>
4003       Owner of the extension
4004      </para></entry>
4005     </row>
4006
4007     <row>
4008      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4009       <structfield>extnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4010       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4011      </para>
4012      <para>
4013       Schema containing the extension's exported objects
4014      </para></entry>
4015     </row>
4016
4017     <row>
4018      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4019       <structfield>extrelocatable</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4020      </para>
4021      <para>
4022       True if extension can be relocated to another schema
4023      </para></entry>
4024     </row>
4025
4026     <row>
4027      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4028       <structfield>extversion</structfield> <type>text</type>
4029      </para>
4030      <para>
4031       Version name for the extension
4032      </para></entry>
4033     </row>
4034
4035     <row>
4036      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4037       <structfield>extconfig</structfield> <type>oid[]</type>
4038       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4039      </para>
4040      <para>
4041       Array of <type>regclass</type> OIDs for the extension's configuration
4042       table(s), or <literal>NULL</literal> if none
4043      </para></entry>
4044     </row>
4045
4046     <row>
4047      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4048       <structfield>extcondition</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
4049      </para>
4050      <para>
4051       Array of <literal>WHERE</literal>-clause filter conditions for the
4052       extension's configuration table(s), or <literal>NULL</literal> if none
4053      </para></entry>
4054     </row>
4055    </tbody>
4056   </tgroup>
4057  </table>
4058
4059  <para>
4060   Note that unlike most catalogs with a <quote>namespace</quote> column,
4061   <structfield>extnamespace</structfield> is not meant to imply
4062   that the extension belongs to that schema.  Extension names are never
4063   schema-qualified.  Rather, <structfield>extnamespace</structfield>
4064   indicates the schema that contains most or all of the extension's
4065   objects.  If <structfield>extrelocatable</structfield> is true, then
4066   this schema must in fact contain all schema-qualifiable objects
4067   belonging to the extension.
4068  </para>
4069 </sect1>
4070
4071
4072 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-foreign-data-wrapper">
4073  <title><structname>pg_foreign_data_wrapper</structname></title>
4074
4075  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-foreign-data-wrapper">
4076   <primary>pg_foreign_data_wrapper</primary>
4077  </indexterm>
4078
4079  <para>
4080   The catalog <structname>pg_foreign_data_wrapper</structname> stores
4081   foreign-data wrapper definitions.  A foreign-data wrapper is the
4082   mechanism by which external data, residing on foreign servers, is
4083   accessed.
4084  </para>
4085
4086  <table>
4087   <title><structname>pg_foreign_data_wrapper</structname> Columns</title>
4088   <tgroup cols="1">
4089    <thead>
4090     <row>
4091      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4092       Column Type
4093      </para>
4094      <para>
4095       Description
4096      </para></entry>
4097     </row>
4098    </thead>
4099
4100    <tbody>
4101     <row>
4102      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4103       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4104      </para>
4105      <para>
4106       Row identifier
4107      </para></entry>
4108     </row>
4109
4110     <row>
4111      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4112       <structfield>fdwname</structfield> <type>name</type>
4113      </para>
4114      <para>
4115       Name of the foreign-data wrapper
4116      </para></entry>
4117     </row>
4118
4119     <row>
4120      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4121       <structfield>fdwowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4122       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4123      </para>
4124      <para>
4125       Owner of the foreign-data wrapper
4126      </para></entry>
4127     </row>
4128
4129     <row>
4130      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4131       <structfield>fdwhandler</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4132       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4133      </para>
4134      <para>
4135       References a handler function that is responsible for
4136       supplying execution routines for the foreign-data wrapper.
4137       Zero if no handler is provided
4138      </para></entry>
4139     </row>
4140
4141     <row>
4142      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4143       <structfield>fdwvalidator</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4144       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4145      </para>
4146      <para>
4147       References a validator function that is responsible for
4148       checking the validity of the options given to the
4149       foreign-data wrapper, as well as options for foreign servers and user
4150       mappings using the foreign-data wrapper.  Zero if no validator
4151       is provided
4152      </para></entry>
4153     </row>
4154
4155     <row>
4156      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4157       <structfield>fdwacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
4158      </para>
4159      <para>
4160       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
4161      </para></entry>
4162     </row>
4163
4164     <row>
4165      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4166       <structfield>fdwoptions</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
4167      </para>
4168      <para>
4169       Foreign-data wrapper specific options, as <quote>keyword=value</quote> strings
4170      </para></entry>
4171     </row>
4172    </tbody>
4173   </tgroup>
4174  </table>
4175 </sect1>
4176
4177
4178 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-foreign-server">
4179  <title><structname>pg_foreign_server</structname></title>
4180
4181  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-foreign-server">
4182   <primary>pg_foreign_server</primary>
4183  </indexterm>
4184
4185  <para>
4186   The catalog <structname>pg_foreign_server</structname> stores
4187   foreign server definitions.  A foreign server describes a source
4188   of external data, such as a remote server.  Foreign
4189   servers are accessed via foreign-data wrappers.
4190  </para>
4191
4192  <table>
4193   <title><structname>pg_foreign_server</structname> Columns</title>
4194   <tgroup cols="1">
4195    <thead>
4196     <row>
4197      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4198       Column Type
4199      </para>
4200      <para>
4201       Description
4202      </para></entry>
4203     </row>
4204    </thead>
4205
4206    <tbody>
4207     <row>
4208      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4209       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4210      </para>
4211      <para>
4212       Row identifier
4213      </para></entry>
4214     </row>
4215
4216     <row>
4217      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4218       <structfield>srvname</structfield> <type>name</type>
4219      </para>
4220      <para>
4221       Name of the foreign server
4222      </para></entry>
4223     </row>
4224
4225     <row>
4226      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4227       <structfield>srvowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4228       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4229      </para>
4230      <para>
4231       Owner of the foreign server
4232      </para></entry>
4233     </row>
4234
4235     <row>
4236      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4237       <structfield>srvfdw</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4238       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-foreign-data-wrapper"><structname>pg_foreign_data_wrapper</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4239      </para>
4240      <para>
4241       OID of the foreign-data wrapper of this foreign server
4242      </para></entry>
4243     </row>
4244
4245     <row>
4246      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4247       <structfield>srvtype</structfield> <type>text</type>
4248      </para>
4249      <para>
4250       Type of the server (optional)
4251      </para></entry>
4252     </row>
4253
4254     <row>
4255      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4256       <structfield>srvversion</structfield> <type>text</type>
4257      </para>
4258      <para>
4259       Version of the server (optional)
4260      </para></entry>
4261     </row>
4262
4263     <row>
4264      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4265       <structfield>srvacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
4266      </para>
4267      <para>
4268       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
4269      </para></entry>
4270     </row>
4271
4272     <row>
4273      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4274       <structfield>srvoptions</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
4275      </para>
4276      <para>
4277       Foreign server specific options, as <quote>keyword=value</quote> strings
4278      </para></entry>
4279     </row>
4280    </tbody>
4281   </tgroup>
4282  </table>
4283 </sect1>
4284
4285
4286 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-foreign-table">
4287  <title><structname>pg_foreign_table</structname></title>
4288
4289  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-foreign-table">
4290   <primary>pg_foreign_table</primary>
4291  </indexterm>
4292
4293  <para>
4294   The catalog <structname>pg_foreign_table</structname> contains
4295   auxiliary information about foreign tables.  A foreign table is
4296   primarily represented by a
4297   <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>
4298   entry, just like a regular table.  Its <structname>pg_foreign_table</structname>
4299   entry contains the information that is pertinent only to foreign tables
4300   and not any other kind of relation.
4301  </para>
4302
4303  <table>
4304   <title><structname>pg_foreign_table</structname> Columns</title>
4305   <tgroup cols="1">
4306    <thead>
4307     <row>
4308      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4309       Column Type
4310      </para>
4311      <para>
4312       Description
4313      </para></entry>
4314     </row>
4315    </thead>
4316
4317    <tbody>
4318     <row>
4319      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4320       <structfield>ftrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4321       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4322      </para>
4323      <para>
4324       The OID of the <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link> entry for this foreign table
4325      </para></entry>
4326     </row>
4327
4328     <row>
4329      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4330       <structfield>ftserver</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4331       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-foreign-server"><structname>pg_foreign_server</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4332      </para>
