4333      <para>
4334       OID of the foreign server for this foreign table
4335      </para></entry>
4336     </row>
4337
4338     <row>
4339      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4340       <structfield>ftoptions</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
4341      </para>
4342      <para>
4343       Foreign table options, as <quote>keyword=value</quote> strings
4344      </para></entry>
4345     </row>
4346    </tbody>
4347   </tgroup>
4348  </table>
4349 </sect1>
4350
4351
4352 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-index">
4353  <title><structname>pg_index</structname></title>
4354
4355  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-index">
4356   <primary>pg_index</primary>
4357  </indexterm>
4358
4359  <para>
4360   The catalog <structname>pg_index</structname> contains part of the information
4361   about indexes.  The rest is mostly in
4362   <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.
4363  </para>
4364
4365  <table>
4366   <title><structname>pg_index</structname> Columns</title>
4367   <tgroup cols="1">
4368    <thead>
4369     <row>
4370      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4371       Column Type
4372      </para>
4373      <para>
4374       Description
4375      </para></entry>
4376     </row>
4377    </thead>
4378
4379    <tbody>
4380     <row>
4381      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4382       <structfield>indexrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4383       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4384      </para>
4385      <para>
4386       The OID of the <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link> entry for this index
4387      </para></entry>
4388     </row>
4389
4390     <row>
4391      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4392       <structfield>indrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4393       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4394      </para>
4395      <para>
4396       The OID of the <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link> entry for the table this index is for
4397      </para></entry>
4398     </row>
4399
4400     <row>
4401      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4402       <structfield>indnatts</structfield> <type>int2</type>
4403      </para>
4404      <para>
4405       The total number of columns in the index (duplicates
4406       <literal>pg_class.relnatts</literal>); this number includes both key and included attributes
4407      </para></entry>
4408     </row>
4409
4410     <row>
4411      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4412       <structfield>indnkeyatts</structfield> <type>int2</type>
4413      </para>
4414      <para>
4415       The number of <firstterm>key columns</firstterm> in the index,
4416       not counting any <firstterm>included columns</firstterm>, which are
4417       merely stored and do not participate in the index semantics
4418      </para></entry>
4419     </row>
4420
4421     <row>
4422      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4423       <structfield>indisunique</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4424      </para>
4425      <para>
4426       If true, this is a unique index
4427      </para></entry>
4428     </row>
4429
4430     <row>
4431      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4432       <structfield>indnullsnotdistinct</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4433      </para>
4434      <para>
4435       This value is only used for unique indexes.  If false, this unique
4436       index will consider null values distinct (so the index can contain
4437       multiple null values in a column, the default PostgreSQL behavior).  If
4438       it is true, it will consider null values to be equal (so the index can
4439       only contain one null value in a column).
4440      </para></entry>
4441     </row>
4442
4443     <row>
4444      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4445       <structfield>indisprimary</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4446      </para>
4447      <para>
4448       If true, this index represents the primary key of the table
4449       (<structfield>indisunique</structfield> should always be true when this is true)
4450      </para></entry>
4451     </row>
4452
4453     <row>
4454      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4455       <structfield>indisexclusion</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4456      </para>
4457      <para>
4458       If true, this index supports an exclusion constraint
4459      </para></entry>
4460     </row>
4461
4462     <row>
4463      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4464       <structfield>indimmediate</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4465      </para>
4466      <para>
4467       If true, the uniqueness check is enforced immediately on
4468       insertion
4469       (irrelevant if <structfield>indisunique</structfield> is not true)
4470      </para></entry>
4471     </row>
4472
4473     <row>
4474      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4475       <structfield>indisclustered</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4476      </para>
4477      <para>
4478       If true, the table was last clustered on this index
4479      </para></entry>
4480     </row>
4481
4482     <row>
4483      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4484       <structfield>indisvalid</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4485      </para>
4486      <para>
4487       If true, the index is currently valid for queries.  False means the
4488       index is possibly incomplete: it must still be modified by
4489       <link linkend="sql-insert"><command>INSERT</command></link>/<link linkend="sql-update"><command>UPDATE</command></link> operations, but it cannot safely
4490       be used for queries. If it is unique, the uniqueness property is not
4491       guaranteed true either.
4492      </para></entry>
4493     </row>
4494
4495     <row>
4496      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4497       <structfield>indcheckxmin</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4498      </para>
4499      <para>
4500       If true, queries must not use the index until the <structfield>xmin</structfield>
4501       of this <structname>pg_index</structname> row is below their <symbol>TransactionXmin</symbol>
4502       event horizon, because the table may contain broken <link linkend="storage-hot">HOT chains</link> with
4503       incompatible rows that they can see
4504      </para></entry>
4505     </row>
4506
4507     <row>
4508      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4509       <structfield>indisready</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4510      </para>
4511      <para>
4512       If true, the index is currently ready for inserts.  False means the
4513       index must be ignored by <link linkend="sql-insert"><command>INSERT</command></link>/<link linkend="sql-update"><command>UPDATE</command></link>
4514       operations.
4515      </para></entry>
4516     </row>
4517
4518     <row>
4519      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4520       <structfield>indislive</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4521      </para>
4522      <para>
4523       If false, the index is in process of being dropped, and should be
4524       ignored for all purposes (including HOT-safety decisions)
4525      </para></entry>
4526     </row>
4527
4528     <row>
4529      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4530       <structfield>indisreplident</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4531      </para>
4532      <para>
4533       If true this index has been chosen as <quote>replica identity</quote>
4534       using <link linkend="sql-altertable-replica-identity"><command>ALTER TABLE ...
4535       REPLICA IDENTITY USING INDEX ...</command></link>
4536      </para></entry>
4537     </row>
4538
4539     <row>
4540      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4541       <structfield>indkey</structfield> <type>int2vector</type>
4542       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link>.<structfield>attnum</structfield>)
4543      </para>
4544      <para>
4545       This is an array of <structfield>indnatts</structfield> values that
4546       indicate which table columns this index indexes.  For example, a value
4547       of <literal>1 3</literal> would mean that the first and the third table
4548       columns make up the index entries.  Key columns come before non-key
4549       (included) columns.  A zero in this array indicates that the
4550       corresponding index attribute is an expression over the table columns,
4551       rather than a simple column reference.
4552      </para></entry>
4553     </row>
4554
4555     <row>
4556      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4557       <structfield>indcollation</structfield> <type>oidvector</type>
4558       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-collation"><structname>pg_collation</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4559      </para>
4560      <para>
4561       For each column in the index key
4562       (<structfield>indnkeyatts</structfield> values), this contains the OID
4563       of the collation to use for the index, or zero if the column is not of
4564       a collatable data type.
4565      </para></entry>
4566     </row>
4567
4568     <row>
4569      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4570       <structfield>indclass</structfield> <type>oidvector</type>
4571       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-opclass"><structname>pg_opclass</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4572      </para>
4573      <para>
4574       For each column in the index key
4575       (<structfield>indnkeyatts</structfield> values), this contains the OID
4576       of the operator class to use.  See
4577       <link linkend="catalog-pg-opclass"><structname>pg_opclass</structname></link> for details.
4578      </para></entry>
4579     </row>
4580
4581     <row>
4582      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4583       <structfield>indoption</structfield> <type>int2vector</type>
4584      </para>
4585      <para>
4586       This is an array of <structfield>indnkeyatts</structfield> values that
4587       store per-column flag bits.  The meaning of the bits is defined by
4588       the index's access method.
4589      </para></entry>
4590     </row>
4591
4592     <row>
4593      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4594       <structfield>indexprs</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
4595      </para>
4596      <para>
4597       Expression trees (in <function>nodeToString()</function>
4598       representation) for index attributes that are not simple column
4599       references.  This is a list with one element for each zero
4600       entry in <structfield>indkey</structfield>.  Null if all index attributes
4601       are simple references.
4602      </para></entry>
4603     </row>
4604
4605     <row>
4606      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4607       <structfield>indpred</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
4608      </para>
4609      <para>
4610       Expression tree (in <function>nodeToString()</function>
4611       representation) for partial index predicate.  Null if not a
4612       partial index.
4613      </para></entry>
4614     </row>
4615    </tbody>
4616   </tgroup>
4617  </table>
4618
4619 </sect1>
4620
4621
4622 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-inherits">
4623  <title><structname>pg_inherits</structname></title>
4624
4625  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-inherits">
4626   <primary>pg_inherits</primary>
4627  </indexterm>
4628
4629  <para>
4630   The catalog <structname>pg_inherits</structname> records information about
4631   table and index inheritance hierarchies.  There is one entry for each direct
4632   parent-child table or index relationship in the database.  (Indirect
4633   inheritance can be determined by following chains of entries.)
4634  </para>
4635
4636  <table>
4637   <title><structname>pg_inherits</structname> Columns</title>
4638   <tgroup cols="1">
4639    <thead>
4640     <row>
4641      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4642       Column Type
4643      </para>
4644      <para>
4645       Description
4646      </para></entry>
4647     </row>
4648    </thead>
4649
4650    <tbody>
4651     <row>
4652      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4653       <structfield>inhrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4654       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4655      </para>
4656      <para>
4657       The OID of the child table or index
4658      </para></entry>
4659     </row>
4660
4661     <row>
4662      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4663       <structfield>inhparent</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4664       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4665      </para>
4666      <para>
4667       The OID of the parent table or index
4668      </para></entry>
4669     </row>
4670
4671     <row>
4672      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4673       <structfield>inhseqno</structfield> <type>int4</type>
4674      </para>
4675      <para>
4676       If there is more than one direct parent for a child table (multiple
4677       inheritance), this number tells the order in which the
4678       inherited columns are to be arranged.  The count starts at 1.
4679      </para>
4680      <para>
4681       Indexes cannot have multiple inheritance, since they can only inherit
4682       when using declarative partitioning.
4683      </para></entry>
4684     </row>
4685
4686     <row>
4687      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4688       <structfield>inhdetachpending</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4689      </para>
4690      <para>
4691       <literal>true</literal> for a partition that is in the process of
4692       being detached; <literal>false</literal> otherwise.
4693      </para></entry>
4694     </row>
4695    </tbody>
4696   </tgroup>
4697  </table>
4698
4699 </sect1>
4700
4701 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-init-privs">
4702  <title><structname>pg_init_privs</structname></title>
4703
4704  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-init-privs">
4705   <primary>pg_init_privs</primary>
4706  </indexterm>
4707
4708  <para>
4709   The catalog <structname>pg_init_privs</structname> records information about
4710   the initial privileges of objects in the system.  There is one entry
4711   for each object in the database which has a non-default (non-NULL)
4712   initial set of privileges.
4713  </para>
4714
4715  <para>
4716   Objects can have initial privileges either by having those privileges set
4717   when the system is initialized (by <application>initdb</application>) or when the
4718   object is created during a <link linkend="sql-createextension"><command>CREATE EXTENSION</command></link> and the
4719   extension script sets initial privileges using the <link linkend="sql-grant"><command>GRANT</command></link>
4720   system.  Note that the system will automatically handle recording of the
4721   privileges during the extension script and that extension authors need
4722   only use the <command>GRANT</command> and <command>REVOKE</command>
4723   statements in their script to have the privileges recorded.  The
4724   <literal>privtype</literal> column indicates if the initial privilege was
4725   set by <application>initdb</application> or during a
4726   <command>CREATE EXTENSION</command> command.
4727  </para>
4728
4729  <para>
4730   Objects which have initial privileges set by <application>initdb</application> will
4731   have entries where <literal>privtype</literal> is
4732   <literal>'i'</literal>, while objects which have initial privileges set
4733   by <command>CREATE EXTENSION</command> will have entries where
4734   <literal>privtype</literal> is <literal>'e'</literal>.
4735  </para>
4736
4737  <table>
4738   <title><structname>pg_init_privs</structname> Columns</title>
4739   <tgroup cols="1">
4740    <thead>
4741     <row>
4742      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4743       Column Type
4744      </para>
4745      <para>
4746       Description
4747      </para></entry>
4748     </row>
4749    </thead>
4750
4751    <tbody>
4752     <row>
4753      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4754       <structfield>objoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4755       (references any OID column)
4756      </para>
4757      <para>
4758       The OID of the specific object
4759      </para></entry>
4760     </row>
4761
4762     <row>
4763      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4764       <structfield>classoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4765       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4766      </para>
4767      <para>
4768       The OID of the system catalog the object is in
4769      </para></entry>
4770     </row>
4771
4772     <row>
4773      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4774       <structfield>objsubid</structfield> <type>int4</type>
4775      </para>
4776      <para>
4777       For a table column, this is the column number (the
4778       <structfield>objoid</structfield> and <structfield>classoid</structfield> refer to the
4779       table itself).  For all other object types, this column is
4780       zero.
4781      </para></entry>
4782     </row>
4783
4784     <row>
4785      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4786       <structfield>privtype</structfield> <type>char</type>
4787      </para>
4788      <para>
4789       A code defining the type of initial privilege of this object; see text
4790      </para></entry>
4791     </row>
4792
4793     <row>
4794      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4795       <structfield>initprivs</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
4796      </para>
4797      <para>
4798       The initial access privileges; see
4799       <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
4800      </para></entry>
4801     </row>
4802    </tbody>
4803   </tgroup>
4804  </table>
4805
4806 </sect1>
4807
4808
4809 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-language">
4810  <title><structname>pg_language</structname></title>
4811
4812  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-language">
4813   <primary>pg_language</primary>
4814  </indexterm>
4815
4816  <para>
4817   The catalog <structname>pg_language</structname> registers
4818   languages in which you can write functions or stored procedures.
4819   See <xref linkend="sql-createlanguage"/>
4820   and <xref linkend="xplang"/> for more information about language handlers.
4821  </para>
4822
4823  <table>
4824   <title><structname>pg_language</structname> Columns</title>
4825   <tgroup cols="1">
4826    <thead>
4827     <row>
4828      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4829       Column Type
4830      </para>
4831      <para>
4832       Description
4833      </para></entry>
4834     </row>
4835    </thead>
4836
4837    <tbody>
4838     <row>
4839      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4840       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4841      </para>
4842      <para>
4843       Row identifier
4844      </para></entry>
4845     </row>
4846
4847     <row>
4848      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4849       <structfield>lanname</structfield> <type>name</type>
4850      </para>
4851      <para>
4852       Name of the language
4853      </para></entry>
4854     </row>
4855
4856     <row>
4857      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4858       <structfield>lanowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4859       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4860      </para>
4861      <para>
4862       Owner of the language
4863      </para></entry>
4864     </row>
4865
4866     <row>
4867      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4868       <structfield>lanispl</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4869      </para>
4870      <para>
4871       This is false for internal languages (such as
4872       <acronym>SQL</acronym>) and true for user-defined languages.
4873       Currently, <application>pg_dump</application> still uses this
4874       to determine which languages need to be dumped, but this might be
4875       replaced by a different mechanism in the future.
4876      </para></entry>
4877     </row>
4878
4879     <row>
4880      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4881       <structfield>lanpltrusted</structfield> <type>bool</type>
4882      </para>
4883      <para>
4884       True if this is a trusted language, which means that it is believed
4885       not to grant access to anything outside the normal SQL execution
4886       environment.  Only superusers can create functions in untrusted
4887       languages.
4888      </para></entry>
4889     </row>
4890
4891     <row>
4892      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4893       <structfield>lanplcallfoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4894       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4895      </para>
4896      <para>
4897       For noninternal languages this references the language
4898       handler, which is a special function that is responsible for
4899       executing all functions that are written in the particular
4900       language. Zero for internal languages.
4901      </para></entry>
4902     </row>
4903
4904     <row>
4905      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4906       <structfield>laninline</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4907       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4908      </para>
4909      <para>
4910       This references a function that is responsible for executing
4911       <quote>inline</quote> anonymous code blocks
4912       (<xref linkend="sql-do"/> blocks).
4913       Zero if inline blocks are not supported.
4914      </para></entry>
4915     </row>
4916
4917     <row>
4918      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4919       <structfield>lanvalidator</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4920       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4921      </para>
4922      <para>
4923       This references a language validator function that is responsible
4924       for checking the syntax and validity of new functions when they
4925       are created.  Zero if no validator is provided.
4926      </para></entry>
4927     </row>
4928
4929     <row>
4930      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4931       <structfield>lanacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
4932      </para>
4933      <para>
4934       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
4935      </para></entry>
4936     </row>
4937    </tbody>
4938   </tgroup>
4939  </table>
4940
4941 </sect1>
4942
4943
4944 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-largeobject">
4945  <title><structname>pg_largeobject</structname></title>
4946
4947  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-largeobject">
4948   <primary>pg_largeobject</primary>
4949  </indexterm>
4950
4951  <para>
4952   The catalog <structname>pg_largeobject</structname> holds the data making up
4953   <quote>large objects</quote>.  A large object is identified by an OID
4954   assigned when it is created.  Each large object is broken into
4955   segments or <quote>pages</quote> small enough to be conveniently stored as rows
4956   in <structname>pg_largeobject</structname>.
4957   The amount of data per page is defined to be <symbol>LOBLKSIZE</symbol> (which is currently
4958   <literal>BLCKSZ/4</literal>, or typically 2 kB).
4959  </para>
4960
4961  <para>
4962   Prior to <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> 9.0, there was no permission structure
4963   associated with large objects.  As a result,
4964   <structname>pg_largeobject</structname> was publicly readable and could be
4965   used to obtain the OIDs (and contents) of all large objects in the system.
4966   This is no longer the case; use
4967   <link linkend="catalog-pg-largeobject-metadata"><structname>pg_largeobject_metadata</structname></link>
4968   to obtain a list of large object OIDs.
4969  </para>
4970
4971  <table>
4972   <title><structname>pg_largeobject</structname> Columns</title>
4973   <tgroup cols="1">
4974    <thead>
4975     <row>
4976      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4977       Column Type
4978      </para>
4979      <para>
4980       Description
4981      </para></entry>
4982     </row>
4983    </thead>
4984
4985    <tbody>
4986     <row>
4987      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4988       <structfield>loid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
4989       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-largeobject-metadata"><structname>pg_largeobject_metadata</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
4990      </para>
4991      <para>
4992       Identifier of the large object that includes this page
4993      </para></entry>
4994     </row>
4995
4996     <row>
4997      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
4998       <structfield>pageno</structfield> <type>int4</type>
4999      </para>
5000      <para>
5001       Page number of this page within its large object
5002       (counting from zero)
5003      </para></entry>
5004     </row>
5005
5006     <row>
5007      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5008       <structfield>data</structfield> <type>bytea</type>
5009      </para>
5010      <para>
5011       Actual data stored in the large object.
5012       This will never be more than <symbol>LOBLKSIZE</symbol> bytes and might be less.
5013      </para></entry>
5014     </row>
5015    </tbody>
5016   </tgroup>
5017  </table>
5018
5019  <para>
5020   Each row of <structname>pg_largeobject</structname> holds data
5021   for one page of a large object, beginning at
5022   byte offset (<literal>pageno * LOBLKSIZE</literal>) within the object.  The implementation
5023   allows sparse storage: pages might be missing, and might be shorter than
5024   <literal>LOBLKSIZE</literal> bytes even if they are not the last page of the object.
5025   Missing regions within a large object read as zeroes.
5026  </para>
5027
5028 </sect1>
5029
5030 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-largeobject-metadata">
5031  <title><structname>pg_largeobject_metadata</structname></title>
5032
5033  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-largeobject-metadata">
5034   <primary>pg_largeobject_metadata</primary>
5035  </indexterm>
5036
5037  <para>
5038   The catalog <structname>pg_largeobject_metadata</structname>
5039   holds metadata associated with large objects.  The actual large object
5040   data is stored in
5041   <link linkend="catalog-pg-largeobject"><structname>pg_largeobject</structname></link>.
5042  </para>
5043
5044  <table>
5045   <title><structname>pg_largeobject_metadata</structname> Columns</title>
5046   <tgroup cols="1">
5047    <thead>
5048     <row>
5049      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5050       Column Type
5051      </para>
5052      <para>
5053       Description
5054      </para></entry>
5055     </row>
5056    </thead>
5057
5058    <tbody>
5059     <row>
5060      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5061       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5062      </para>
5063      <para>
5064       Row identifier
5065      </para></entry>
5066     </row>
5067
5068     <row>
5069      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5070       <structfield>lomowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5071       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5072      </para>
5073      <para>
5074       Owner of the large object
5075      </para></entry>
5076     </row>
5077
5078     <row>
5079      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5080       <structfield>lomacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
5081      </para>
5082      <para>
5083       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
5084      </para></entry>
5085     </row>
5086    </tbody>
5087   </tgroup>
5088  </table>
5089 </sect1>
5090
5091
5092 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-namespace">
5093  <title><structname>pg_namespace</structname></title>
5094
5095  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-namespace">
5096   <primary>pg_namespace</primary>
5097  </indexterm>
5098
5099  <para>
5100   The catalog <structname>pg_namespace</structname> stores namespaces.
5101   A namespace is the structure underlying SQL schemas: each namespace
5102   can have a separate collection of relations, types, etc. without name
5103   conflicts.
5104  </para>
5105
5106  <table>
5107   <title><structname>pg_namespace</structname> Columns</title>
5108   <tgroup cols="1">
5109    <thead>
5110     <row>
5111      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5112       Column Type
5113      </para>
5114      <para>
5115       Description
5116      </para></entry>
5117     </row>
5118    </thead>
5119
5120    <tbody>
5121     <row>
5122      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5123       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5124      </para>
5125      <para>
5126       Row identifier
5127      </para></entry>
5128     </row>
5129
5130     <row>
5131      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5132       <structfield>nspname</structfield> <type>name</type>
5133      </para>
5134      <para>
5135       Name of the namespace
5136      </para></entry>
5137     </row>
5138
5139     <row>
5140      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5141       <structfield>nspowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5142       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5143      </para>
5144      <para>
5145       Owner of the namespace
5146      </para></entry>
5147     </row>
5148
5149     <row>
5150      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5151       <structfield>nspacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
5152      </para>
5153      <para>
5154       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
5155      </para></entry>
5156     </row>
5157    </tbody>
5158   </tgroup>
5159  </table>
5160
5161 </sect1>
5162
5163
5164 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-opclass">
5165  <title><structname>pg_opclass</structname></title>
5166
5167  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-opclass">
5168   <primary>pg_opclass</primary>
5169  </indexterm>
5170
5171  <para>
5172   The catalog <structname>pg_opclass</structname> defines
5173   index access method operator classes.  Each operator class defines
5174   semantics for index columns of a particular data type and a particular
5175   index access method.  An operator class essentially specifies that a
5176   particular operator family is applicable to a particular indexable column
5177   data type.  The set of operators from the family that are actually usable
5178   with the indexed column are whichever ones accept the column's data type
5179   as their left-hand input.
5180  </para>
5181
5182  <para>
5183   Operator classes are described at length in <xref linkend="xindex"/>.
5184  </para>
5185
5186  <table>
5187   <title><structname>pg_opclass</structname> Columns</title>
5188   <tgroup cols="1">
5189    <thead>
5190     <row>
5191      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5192       Column Type
5193      </para>
5194      <para>
5195       Description
5196      </para></entry>
5197     </row>
5198    </thead>
5199
5200    <tbody>
5201     <row>
5202      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5203       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5204      </para>
5205      <para>
5206       Row identifier
5207      </para></entry>
5208     </row>
5209
5210     <row>
5211      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5212       <structfield>opcmethod</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5213       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-am"><structname>pg_am</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5214      </para>
5215      <para>
5216       Index access method operator class is for
5217      </para></entry>
5218     </row>
5219
5220     <row>
5221      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5222       <structfield>opcname</structfield> <type>name</type>
5223      </para>
5224      <para>
5225       Name of this operator class
5226      </para></entry>
5227     </row>
5228
5229     <row>
5230      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5231       <structfield>opcnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5232       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5233      </para>
5234      <para>
5235       Namespace of this operator class
5236      </para></entry>
5237     </row>
5238
5239     <row>
5240      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5241       <structfield>opcowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5242       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5243      </para>
5244      <para>
5245       Owner of the operator class
5246      </para></entry>
5247     </row>
5248
5249     <row>
5250      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5251       <structfield>opcfamily</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5252       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-opfamily"><structname>pg_opfamily</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5253      </para>
5254      <para>
5255       Operator family containing the operator class
5256      </para></entry>
5257     </row>
5258
5259     <row>
5260      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5261       <structfield>opcintype</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5262       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5263      </para>
5264      <para>
5265       Data type that the operator class indexes
5266      </para></entry>
5267     </row>
5268
5269     <row>
5270      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5271       <structfield>opcdefault</structfield> <type>bool</type>
5272      </para>
5273      <para>
5274       True if this operator class is the default for <structfield>opcintype</structfield>
5275      </para></entry>
5276     </row>
5277
5278     <row>
5279      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5280       <structfield>opckeytype</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5281       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5282      </para>
5283      <para>
5284       Type of data stored in index, or zero if same as <structfield>opcintype</structfield>
5285      </para></entry>
5286     </row>
5287    </tbody>
5288   </tgroup>
5289  </table>
5290
5291  <para>
5292   An operator class's <structfield>opcmethod</structfield> must match the
5293   <structfield>opfmethod</structfield> of its containing operator family.
5294   Also, there must be no more than one <structname>pg_opclass</structname>
5295   row having <structfield>opcdefault</structfield> true for any given combination of
5296   <structfield>opcmethod</structfield> and <structfield>opcintype</structfield>.
5297  </para>
5298
5299 </sect1>
5300
5301
5302 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-operator">
5303  <title><structname>pg_operator</structname></title>
5304
5305  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-operator">
5306   <primary>pg_operator</primary>
5307  </indexterm>
5308
5309  <para>
5310   The catalog <structname>pg_operator</structname> stores information about operators.
5311   See <xref linkend="sql-createoperator"/>
5312   and <xref linkend="xoper"/> for more information.
5313  </para>
5314
5315  <table>
5316   <title><structname>pg_operator</structname> Columns</title>
5317   <tgroup cols="1">
5318    <thead>
5319     <row>
5320      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5321       Column Type
5322      </para>
5323      <para>
5324       Description
5325      </para></entry>
5326     </row>
5327    </thead>
5328
5329    <tbody>
5330     <row>
5331      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5332       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5333      </para>
5334      <para>
5335       Row identifier
5336      </para></entry>
5337     </row>
5338
5339     <row>
5340      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5341       <structfield>oprname</structfield> <type>name</type>
5342      </para>
5343      <para>
5344       Name of the operator
5345      </para></entry>
5346     </row>
5347
5348     <row>
5349      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5350       <structfield>oprnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5351       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5352      </para>
5353      <para>
5354       The OID of the namespace that contains this operator
5355      </para></entry>
5356     </row>
5357
5358     <row>
5359      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5360       <structfield>oprowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5361       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5362      </para>
5363      <para>
5364       Owner of the operator
5365      </para></entry>
5366     </row>
5367
5368     <row>
5369      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5370       <structfield>oprkind</structfield> <type>char</type>
5371      </para>
5372      <para>
5373       <literal>b</literal> = infix operator (<quote>both</quote>),
5374       or <literal>l</literal> = prefix operator (<quote>left</quote>)
5375      </para></entry>
5376     </row>
5377
5378     <row>
5379      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5380       <structfield>oprcanmerge</structfield> <type>bool</type>
5381      </para>
5382      <para>
5383       This operator supports merge joins
5384      </para></entry>
5385     </row>
5386
5387     <row>
5388      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5389       <structfield>oprcanhash</structfield> <type>bool</type>
5390      </para>
5391      <para>
5392       This operator supports hash joins
5393      </para></entry>
5394     </row>
5395
5396     <row>
5397      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5398       <structfield>oprleft</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5399       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5400      </para>
5401      <para>
5402       Type of the left operand (zero for a prefix operator)
5403      </para></entry>
5404     </row>
5405
5406     <row>
5407      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5408       <structfield>oprright</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5409       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5410      </para>
5411      <para>
5412       Type of the right operand
5413      </para></entry>
5414     </row>
5415
5416     <row>
5417      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5418       <structfield>oprresult</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5419       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5420      </para>
5421      <para>
5422       Type of the result
5423       (zero for a not-yet-defined <quote>shell</quote> operator)
5424      </para></entry>
5425     </row>
5426
5427     <row>
5428      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5429       <structfield>oprcom</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5430       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-operator"><structname>pg_operator</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5431      </para>
5432      <para>
5433       Commutator of this operator (zero if none)
5434      </para></entry>
5435     </row>
5436
5437     <row>
5438      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5439       <structfield>oprnegate</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5440       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-operator"><structname>pg_operator</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5441      </para>
5442      <para>
5443       Negator of this operator (zero if none)
5444      </para></entry>
5445     </row>
5446
5447     <row>
5448      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5449       <structfield>oprcode</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
5450       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5451      </para>
5452      <para>
5453       Function that implements this operator
5454       (zero for a not-yet-defined <quote>shell</quote> operator)
5455      </para></entry>
5456     </row>
5457
5458     <row>
5459      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5460       <structfield>oprrest</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
5461       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5462      </para>
5463      <para>
5464       Restriction selectivity estimation function for this operator
5465       (zero if none)
5466      </para></entry>
5467     </row>
5468
5469     <row>
5470      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5471       <structfield>oprjoin</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
5472       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5473      </para>
5474      <para>
5475       Join selectivity estimation function for this operator
5476       (zero if none)
5477      </para></entry>
5478     </row>
5479    </tbody>
5480   </tgroup>
5481  </table>
5482
5483 </sect1>
5484
5485
5486 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-opfamily">
5487  <title><structname>pg_opfamily</structname></title>
5488
5489  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-opfamily">
5490   <primary>pg_opfamily</primary>
5491  </indexterm>
5492
5493  <para>
5494   The catalog <structname>pg_opfamily</structname> defines operator families.
5495   Each operator family is a collection of operators and associated
5496   support routines that implement the semantics specified for a particular
5497   index access method.  Furthermore, the operators in a family are all
5498   <quote>compatible</quote>, in a way that is specified by the access method.
5499   The operator family concept allows cross-data-type operators to be used
5500   with indexes and to be reasoned about using knowledge of access method
5501   semantics.
5502  </para>
5503
5504  <para>
5505   Operator families are described at length in <xref linkend="xindex"/>.
5506  </para>
5507
5508  <table>
5509   <title><structname>pg_opfamily</structname> Columns</title>
5510   <tgroup cols="1">
5511    <thead>
5512     <row>
5513      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5514       Column Type
5515      </para>
5516      <para>
5517       Description
5518      </para></entry>
5519     </row>
5520    </thead>
5521
5522    <tbody>
5523     <row>
5524      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5525       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5526      </para>
5527      <para>
5528       Row identifier
5529      </para></entry>
5530     </row>
5531
5532     <row>
5533      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5534       <structfield>opfmethod</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5535       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-am"><structname>pg_am</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5536      </para>
5537      <para>
5538       Index access method operator family is for
5539      </para></entry>
5540     </row>
5541
5542     <row>
5543      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5544       <structfield>opfname</structfield> <type>name</type>
5545      </para>
5546      <para>
5547       Name of this operator family
5548      </para></entry>
5549     </row>
5550
5551     <row>
5552      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5553       <structfield>opfnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5554       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5555      </para>
5556      <para>
5557       Namespace of this operator family
5558      </para></entry>
5559     </row>
5560
5561     <row>
5562      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5563       <structfield>opfowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5564       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5565      </para>
5566      <para>
5567       Owner of the operator family
5568      </para></entry>
5569     </row>
5570    </tbody>
5571   </tgroup>
5572  </table>
5573
5574  <para>
5575   The majority of the information defining an operator family is not in its
5576   <structname>pg_opfamily</structname> row, but in the associated rows in
5577   <link linkend="catalog-pg-amop"><structname>pg_amop</structname></link>,
5578   <link linkend="catalog-pg-amproc"><structname>pg_amproc</structname></link>,
5579   and
5580   <link linkend="catalog-pg-opclass"><structname>pg_opclass</structname></link>.
5581  </para>
5582
5583 </sect1>
5584
5585
5586 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-parameter-acl">
5587  <title><structname>pg_parameter_acl</structname></title>
5588
5589  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-parameter-acl">
5590   <primary>pg_parameter_acl</primary>
5591  </indexterm>
5592
5593  <para>
5594   The catalog <structname>pg_parameter_acl</structname> records configuration
5595   parameters for which privileges have been granted to one or more roles.
5596   No entry is made for parameters that have default privileges.
5597  </para>
5598
5599  <para>
5600   Unlike most system catalogs, <structname>pg_parameter_acl</structname>
5601   is shared across all databases of a cluster: there is only one
5602   copy of <structname>pg_parameter_acl</structname> per cluster, not
5603   one per database.
5604  </para>
5605
5606  <table>
5607   <title><structname>pg_parameter_acl</structname> Columns</title>
5608   <tgroup cols="1">
5609    <thead>
5610     <row>
5611      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5612       Column Type
5613      </para>
5614      <para>
5615       Description
5616      </para></entry>
5617     </row>
5618    </thead>
5619
5620    <tbody>
5621     <row>
5622      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5623       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5624      </para>
5625      <para>
5626       Row identifier
5627      </para></entry>
5628     </row>
5629
5630     <row>
5631      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5632       <structfield>parname</structfield> <type>text</type>
5633      </para>
5634      <para>
5635       The name of a configuration parameter for which privileges are granted
5636      </para></entry>
5637     </row>
5638
5639     <row>
5640      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5641       <structfield>paracl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
5642      </para>
5643      <para>
5644       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
5645      </para></entry>
5646     </row>
5647
5648    </tbody>
5649   </tgroup>
5650  </table>
5651 </sect1>
5652
5653
5654 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-partitioned-table">
5655  <title><structname>pg_partitioned_table</structname></title>
5656
5657  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-partitioned-table">
5658   <primary>pg_partitioned_table</primary>
5659  </indexterm>
5660
5661  <para>
5662   The catalog <structname>pg_partitioned_table</structname> stores
5663   information about how tables are partitioned.
5664  </para>
5665
5666  <table>
5667   <title><structname>pg_partitioned_table</structname> Columns</title>
5668   <tgroup cols="1">
5669    <thead>
5670     <row>
5671      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5672       Column Type
5673      </para>
5674      <para>
5675       Description
5676      </para></entry>
5677     </row>
5678    </thead>
5679
5680    <tbody>
5681     <row>
5682      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5683       <structfield>partrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5684       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5685      </para>
5686      <para>
5687       The OID of the <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link> entry for this partitioned table
5688      </para></entry>
5689     </row>
5690
5691     <row>
5692      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5693       <structfield>partstrat</structfield> <type>char</type>
5694      </para>
5695      <para>
5696       Partitioning strategy; <literal>h</literal> = hash partitioned table,
5697       <literal>l</literal> = list partitioned table, <literal>r</literal> = range partitioned table
5698      </para></entry>
5699     </row>
5700
5701     <row>
5702      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5703       <structfield>partnatts</structfield> <type>int2</type>
5704      </para>
5705      <para>
5706       The number of columns in the partition key
5707      </para></entry>
5708     </row>
5709
5710     <row>
5711      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5712       <structfield>partdefid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5713       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5714      </para>
5715      <para>
5716       The OID of the <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link> entry for the default partition
5717       of this partitioned table, or zero if this partitioned table does not
5718       have a default partition
5719      </para></entry>
5720     </row>
5721
5722     <row>
5723      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5724       <structfield>partattrs</structfield> <type>int2vector</type>
5725       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link>.<structfield>attnum</structfield>)
5726      </para>
5727      <para>
5728       This is an array of <structfield>partnatts</structfield> values that
5729       indicate which table columns are part of the partition key.  For
5730       example, a value of <literal>1 3</literal> would mean that the first
5731       and the third table columns make up the partition key.  A zero in this
5732       array indicates that the corresponding partition key column is an
5733       expression, rather than a simple column reference.
5734      </para></entry>
5735     </row>
5736
5737     <row>
5738      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5739       <structfield>partclass</structfield> <type>oidvector</type>
5740       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-opclass"><structname>pg_opclass</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5741      </para>
5742      <para>
5743       For each column in the partition key, this contains the OID of the
5744       operator class to use.  See
5745       <link linkend="catalog-pg-opclass"><structname>pg_opclass</structname></link> for details.
5746      </para></entry>
5747     </row>
5748
5749     <row>
5750      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5751       <structfield>partcollation</structfield> <type>oidvector</type>
5752       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-collation"><structname>pg_collation</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5753      </para>
5754      <para>
5755       For each column in the partition key, this contains the OID of the
5756       collation to use for partitioning, or zero if the column is not
5757       of a collatable data type.
5758      </para></entry>
5759     </row>
5760
5761     <row>
5762      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5763       <structfield>partexprs</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
5764      </para>
5765      <para>
5766       Expression trees (in <function>nodeToString()</function>
5767       representation) for partition key columns that are not simple column
5768       references.  This is a list with one element for each zero
5769       entry in <structfield>partattrs</structfield>.  Null if all partition key columns
5770       are simple references.
5771      </para></entry>
5772     </row>
5773    </tbody>
5774   </tgroup>
5775  </table>
5776 </sect1>
5777
5778
5779 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-policy">
5780  <title><structname>pg_policy</structname></title>
5781
5782  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-policy">
5783   <primary>pg_policy</primary>
5784  </indexterm>
5785
5786  <para>
5787   The catalog <structname>pg_policy</structname> stores row-level
5788   security policies for tables.  A policy includes the kind of
5789   command that it applies to (possibly all commands), the roles that it
5790   applies to, the expression to be added as a security-barrier
5791   qualification to queries that include the table, and the expression
5792   to be added as a <literal>WITH CHECK</literal> option for queries that attempt to
5793   add new records to the table.
5794  </para>
5795
5796  <table>
5797   <title><structname>pg_policy</structname> Columns</title>
5798   <tgroup cols="1">
5799    <thead>
5800     <row>
5801      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5802       Column Type
5803      </para>
5804      <para>
5805       Description
5806      </para></entry>
5807     </row>
5808    </thead>
5809
5810    <tbody>
5811     <row>
5812      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5813       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5814      </para>
5815      <para>
5816       Row identifier
5817      </para></entry>
5818     </row>
5819
5820     <row>
5821      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5822       <structfield>polname</structfield> <type>name</type>
5823      </para>
5824      <para>
5825       The name of the policy
5826      </para></entry>
5827     </row>
5828
5829     <row>
5830      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5831       <structfield>polrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5832       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5833      </para>
5834      <para>
5835       The table to which the policy applies
5836      </para></entry>
5837     </row>
5838
5839     <row>
5840      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5841       <structfield>polcmd</structfield> <type>char</type>
5842      </para>
5843      <para>
5844       The command type to which the policy is applied:
5845       <literal>r</literal> for <xref linkend="sql-select"/>,
5846       <literal>a</literal> for <xref linkend="sql-insert"/>,
5847       <literal>w</literal> for <xref linkend="sql-update"/>,
5848       <literal>d</literal> for <xref linkend="sql-delete"/>,
5849       or <literal>*</literal> for all
5850      </para></entry>
5851     </row>
5852
5853     <row>
5854      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5855       <structfield>polpermissive</structfield> <type>bool</type>
5856      </para>
5857      <para>
5858       Is the policy permissive or restrictive?
5859      </para></entry>
5860     </row>
5861
5862     <row>
5863      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5864       <structfield>polroles</structfield> <type>oid[]</type>
5865       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5866      </para>
5867      <para>
5868       The roles to which the policy is applied;
5869       zero means <literal>PUBLIC</literal>
5870       (and normally appears alone in the array)
5871      </para></entry>
5872     </row>
5873
5874     <row>
5875      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5876       <structfield>polqual</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
5877      </para>
5878      <para>
5879       The expression tree to be added to the security barrier qualifications for queries that use the table
5880      </para></entry>
5881     </row>
5882
5883     <row>
5884      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5885       <structfield>polwithcheck</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
5886      </para>
5887      <para>
5888       The expression tree to be added to the WITH CHECK qualifications for queries that attempt to add rows to the table
5889      </para></entry>
5890     </row>
5891    </tbody>
5892   </tgroup>
5893  </table>
5894
5895  <note>
5896   <para>
5897    Policies stored in <structname>pg_policy</structname> are applied only when
5898    <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>relrowsecurity</structfield> is set for
5899    their table.
5900   </para>
5901  </note>
5902
5903 </sect1>
5904
5905 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-proc">
5906  <title><structname>pg_proc</structname></title>
5907
5908  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-proc">
5909   <primary>pg_proc</primary>
5910  </indexterm>
5911
5912  <para>
5913   The catalog <structname>pg_proc</structname> stores information about
5914   functions, procedures, aggregate functions, and window functions
5915   (collectively also known as routines).  See <xref
5916   linkend="sql-createfunction"/>, <xref linkend="sql-createprocedure"/>, and
5917   <xref linkend="xfunc"/> for more information.
5918  </para>
5919
5920  <para>
5921   If <structfield>prokind</structfield> indicates that the entry is for an
5922   aggregate function, there should be a matching row in
5923   <link linkend="catalog-pg-aggregate"><structfield>pg_aggregate</structfield></link>.
5924  </para>
5925
5926  <table>
5927   <title><structname>pg_proc</structname> Columns</title>
5928   <tgroup cols="1">
5929    <thead>
5930     <row>
5931      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5932       Column Type
5933      </para>
5934      <para>
5935       Description
5936      </para></entry>
5937     </row>
5938    </thead>
5939
5940    <tbody>
5941     <row>
5942      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5943       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5944      </para>
5945      <para>
5946       Row identifier
5947      </para></entry>
5948     </row>
5949
5950     <row>
5951      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5952       <structfield>proname</structfield> <type>name</type>
5953      </para>
5954      <para>
5955       Name of the function
5956      </para></entry>
5957     </row>
5958
5959     <row>
5960      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5961       <structfield>pronamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5962       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5963      </para>
5964      <para>
5965       The OID of the namespace that contains this function
5966      </para></entry>
5967     </row>
5968
5969     <row>
5970      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5971       <structfield>proowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5972       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5973      </para>
5974      <para>
5975       Owner of the function
5976      </para></entry>
5977     </row>
5978
5979     <row>
5980      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5981       <structfield>prolang</structfield> <type>oid</type>
5982       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-language"><structname>pg_language</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
5983      </para>
5984      <para>
5985       Implementation language or call interface of this function
5986      </para></entry>
5987     </row>
5988
5989     <row>
5990      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
5991       <structfield>procost</structfield> <type>float4</type>
5992      </para>
5993      <para>
5994       Estimated execution cost (in units of
5995       <xref linkend="guc-cpu-operator-cost"/>); if <structfield>proretset</structfield>,
5996       this is cost per row returned
5997      </para></entry>
5998     </row>
5999
6000     <row>
6001      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6002       <structfield>prorows</structfield> <type>float4</type>
6003      </para>
6004      <para>
6005       Estimated number of result rows (zero if not <structfield>proretset</structfield>)
6006      </para></entry>
6007     </row>
6008
6009     <row>
6010      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6011       <structfield>provariadic</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6012       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6013      </para>
6014      <para>
6015       Data type of the variadic array parameter's elements,
6016       or zero if the function does not have a variadic parameter
6017      </para></entry>
6018     </row>
6019
6020     <row>
6021      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6022       <structfield>prosupport</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
6023       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6024      </para>
6025      <para>
6026       Planner support function for this function
6027       (see <xref linkend="xfunc-optimization"/>), or zero if none
6028      </para></entry>
6029     </row>
6030
6031     <row>
6032      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6033       <structfield>prokind</structfield> <type>char</type>
6034      </para>
6035      <para>
6036       <literal>f</literal> for a normal function, <literal>p</literal>
6037       for a procedure, <literal>a</literal> for an aggregate function, or
6038       <literal>w</literal> for a window function
6039      </para></entry>
6040     </row>
6041
6042     <row>
6043      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6044       <structfield>prosecdef</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6045      </para>
6046      <para>
6047       Function is a security definer (i.e., a <quote>setuid</quote>
6048       function)
6049      </para></entry>
6050     </row>
6051
6052     <row>
6053      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6054       <structfield>proleakproof</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6055      </para>
6056      <para>
6057       The function has no side effects.  No information about the
6058       arguments is conveyed except via the return value.  Any function
6059       that might throw an error depending on the values of its arguments
6060       is not leakproof.
6061      </para></entry>
6062     </row>
6063
6064     <row>
6065      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6066       <structfield>proisstrict</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6067      </para>
6068      <para>
6069       Function returns null if any call argument is null.  In that
6070       case the function won't actually be called at all.  Functions
6071       that are not <quote>strict</quote> must be prepared to handle
6072       null inputs.
6073      </para></entry>
6074     </row>
6075
6076     <row>
6077      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6078       <structfield>proretset</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6079      </para>
6080      <para>
6081       Function returns a set (i.e., multiple values of the specified
6082       data type)
6083      </para></entry>
6084     </row>
6085
6086     <row>
6087      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6088       <structfield>provolatile</structfield> <type>char</type>
6089      </para>
6090      <para>
6091       <structfield>provolatile</structfield> tells whether the function's
6092       result depends only on its input arguments, or is affected by outside
6093       factors.
6094       It is <literal>i</literal> for <quote>immutable</quote> functions,
6095       which always deliver the same result for the same inputs.
6096       It is <literal>s</literal> for <quote>stable</quote> functions,
6097       whose results (for fixed inputs) do not change within a scan.
6098       It is <literal>v</literal> for <quote>volatile</quote> functions,
6099       whose results might change at any time.  (Use <literal>v</literal> also
6100       for functions with side-effects, so that calls to them cannot get
6101       optimized away.)
6102      </para></entry>
6103     </row>
6104
6105     <row>
6106      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6107       <structfield>proparallel</structfield> <type>char</type>
6108      </para>
6109      <para>
6110       <structfield>proparallel</structfield> tells whether the function
6111       can be safely run in parallel mode.
6112       It is <literal>s</literal> for functions which are safe to run in
6113       parallel mode without restriction.
6114       It is <literal>r</literal> for functions which can be run in parallel
6115       mode, but their execution is restricted to the parallel group leader;
6116       parallel worker processes cannot invoke these functions.
6117       It is <literal>u</literal> for functions which are unsafe in parallel
6118       mode; the presence of such a function forces a serial execution plan.
6119      </para></entry>
6120     </row>
6121
6122     <row>
6123      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6124       <structfield>pronargs</structfield> <type>int2</type>
6125      </para>
6126      <para>
6127       Number of input arguments
6128      </para></entry>
6129     </row>
6130
6131     <row>
6132      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6133       <structfield>pronargdefaults</structfield> <type>int2</type>
6134      </para>
6135      <para>
6136       Number of arguments that have defaults
6137      </para></entry>
6138     </row>
6139
6140     <row>
6141      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6142       <structfield>prorettype</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6143       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6144      </para>
6145      <para>
6146       Data type of the return value
6147      </para></entry>
6148     </row>
6149
6150     <row>
6151      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6152       <structfield>proargtypes</structfield> <type>oidvector</type>
6153       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6154      </para>
6155      <para>
6156       An array of the data types of the function arguments.  This includes
6157       only input arguments (including <literal>INOUT</literal> and
6158       <literal>VARIADIC</literal> arguments), and thus represents
6159       the call signature of the function.
6160      </para></entry>
6161     </row>
6162
6163     <row>
6164      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6165       <structfield>proallargtypes</structfield> <type>oid[]</type>
6166       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6167      </para>
6168      <para>
6169       An array of the data types of the function arguments.  This includes
6170       all arguments (including <literal>OUT</literal> and
6171       <literal>INOUT</literal> arguments); however, if all the
6172       arguments are <literal>IN</literal> arguments, this field will be null.
6173       Note that subscripting is 1-based, whereas for historical reasons
6174       <structfield>proargtypes</structfield> is subscripted from 0.
6175      </para></entry>
6176     </row>
6177
6178     <row>
6179      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6180       <structfield>proargmodes</structfield> <type>char[]</type>
6181      </para>
6182      <para>
6183       An array of the modes of the function arguments, encoded as
6184       <literal>i</literal> for <literal>IN</literal> arguments,
6185       <literal>o</literal> for <literal>OUT</literal> arguments,
6186       <literal>b</literal> for <literal>INOUT</literal> arguments,
6187       <literal>v</literal> for <literal>VARIADIC</literal> arguments,
6188       <literal>t</literal> for <literal>TABLE</literal> arguments.
6189       If all the arguments are <literal>IN</literal> arguments,
6190       this field will be null.
6191       Note that subscripts correspond to positions of
6192       <structfield>proallargtypes</structfield> not <structfield>proargtypes</structfield>.
6193      </para></entry>
6194     </row>
6195
6196     <row>
6197      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6198       <structfield>proargnames</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
6199      </para>
6200      <para>
6201       An array of the names of the function arguments.
6202       Arguments without a name are set to empty strings in the array.
6203       If none of the arguments have a name, this field will be null.
6204       Note that subscripts correspond to positions of
6205       <structfield>proallargtypes</structfield> not <structfield>proargtypes</structfield>.
6206      </para></entry>
6207     </row>
6208
6209     <row>
6210      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6211       <structfield>proargdefaults</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
6212      </para>
6213      <para>
6214       Expression trees (in <function>nodeToString()</function> representation)
6215       for default values.  This is a list with
6216       <structfield>pronargdefaults</structfield> elements, corresponding to the last
6217       <replaceable>N</replaceable> <emphasis>input</emphasis> arguments (i.e., the last
6218       <replaceable>N</replaceable> <structfield>proargtypes</structfield> positions).
6219       If none of the arguments have defaults, this field will be null.
6220      </para></entry>
6221     </row>
6222
6223     <row>
6224      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6225       <structfield>protrftypes</structfield> <type>oid[]</type>
6226       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6227      </para>
6228      <para>
6229       An array of the argument/result data type(s) for which to apply
6230       transforms (from the function's <literal>TRANSFORM</literal>
6231       clause).  Null if none.
6232      </para></entry>
6233     </row>
6234
6235     <row>
6236      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6237       <structfield>prosrc</structfield> <type>text</type>
6238      </para>
6239      <para>
6240       This tells the function handler how to invoke the function.  It
6241       might be the actual source code of the function for interpreted
6242       languages, a link symbol, a file name, or just about anything
6243       else, depending on the implementation language/call convention.
6244      </para></entry>
6245     </row>
6246
6247     <row>
6248      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6249       <structfield>probin</structfield> <type>text</type>
6250      </para>
6251      <para>
6252       Additional information about how to invoke the function.
6253       Again, the interpretation is language-specific.
6254      </para></entry>
6255     </row>
6256
6257     <row>
6258      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6259       <structfield>prosqlbody</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
6260      </para>
6261      <para>
6262       Pre-parsed SQL function body.  This is used for SQL-language
6263       functions when the body is given in SQL-standard notation
6264       rather than as a string literal.  It's null in other cases.
6265       </para></entry>
6266     </row>
6267
6268     <row>
6269      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6270       <structfield>proconfig</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
6271      </para>
6272      <para>
6273       Function's local settings for run-time configuration variables
6274      </para></entry>
6275     </row>
6276
6277     <row>
6278      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6279       <structfield>proacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
6280      </para>
6281      <para>
6282       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
6283      </para></entry>
6284     </row>
6285    </tbody>
6286   </tgroup>
6287  </table>
6288
6289  <para>
6290   For compiled functions, both built-in and dynamically loaded,
6291   <structfield>prosrc</structfield> contains the function's C-language
6292   name (link symbol).
6293   For SQL-language functions, <structfield>prosrc</structfield> contains
6294   the function's source text if that is specified as a string literal;
6295   but if the function body is specified in SQL-standard style,
6296   <structfield>prosrc</structfield> is unused (typically it's an empty
6297   string) and <structfield>prosqlbody</structfield> contains the
6298   pre-parsed definition.
6299   For all other currently-known language types,
6300   <structfield>prosrc</structfield> contains the function's source
6301   text.  <structfield>probin</structfield> is null except for
6302   dynamically-loaded C functions, for which it gives the name of the
6303   shared library file containing the function.
6304  </para>
6305
6306 </sect1>
6307
6308 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-publication">
6309  <title><structname>pg_publication</structname></title>
6310
6311  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-publication">
6312   <primary>pg_publication</primary>
6313  </indexterm>
6314
6315  <para>
6316   The catalog <structname>pg_publication</structname> contains all
6317   publications created in the database.  For more on publications see
6318   <xref linkend="logical-replication-publication"/>.
6319  </para>
6320
6321  <table>
6322   <title><structname>pg_publication</structname> Columns</title>
6323   <tgroup cols="1">
6324    <thead>
6325     <row>
6326      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6327       Column Type
6328      </para>
6329      <para>
6330       Description
6331      </para></entry>
6332     </row>
6333    </thead>
6334
6335    <tbody>
6336     <row>
6337      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6338       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6339      </para>
6340      <para>
6341       Row identifier
6342      </para></entry>
6343     </row>
6344
6345     <row>
6346      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6347       <structfield>pubname</structfield> <type>name</type>
6348      </para>
6349      <para>
6350       Name of the publication
6351      </para></entry>
6352     </row>
6353
6354     <row>
6355      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6356       <structfield>pubowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6357       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6358      </para>
6359      <para>
6360       Owner of the publication
6361      </para></entry>
6362     </row>
6363
6364     <row>
6365      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6366       <structfield>puballtables</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6367      </para>
6368      <para>
6369       If true, this publication automatically includes all tables
6370       in the database, including any that will be created in the future.
6371      </para></entry>
6372     </row>
6373
6374     <row>
6375      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6376       <structfield>pubinsert</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6377      </para>
6378      <para>
6379       If true, <xref linkend="sql-insert"/> operations are replicated for
6380       tables in the publication.
6381      </para></entry>
6382     </row>
6383
6384     <row>
6385      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6386       <structfield>pubupdate</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6387      </para>
6388      <para>
6389       If true, <xref linkend="sql-update"/> operations are replicated for
6390       tables in the publication.
6391      </para></entry>
6392     </row>
6393
6394     <row>
6395      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6396       <structfield>pubdelete</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6397      </para>
6398      <para>
6399       If true, <xref linkend="sql-delete"/> operations are replicated for
6400       tables in the publication.
6401      </para></entry>
6402     </row>
6403
6404     <row>
6405      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6406       <structfield>pubtruncate</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6407      </para>
6408      <para>
6409       If true, <xref linkend="sql-truncate"/> operations are replicated for
6410       tables in the publication.
6411      </para></entry>
6412     </row>
6413
6414     <row>
6415      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6416       <structfield>pubviaroot</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6417      </para>
6418      <para>
6419       If true, operations on a leaf partition are replicated using the
6420       identity and schema of its topmost partitioned ancestor mentioned in the
6421       publication instead of its own.
6422      </para></entry>
