6423     </row>
6424
6425     <row>
6426      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6427       <structfield>pubgencols</structfield> <type>char</type>
6428      </para>
6429      <para>
6430       Controls how to handle generated column replication when there is no
6431       publication column list:
6432       <literal>n</literal> = generated columns in the tables associated with
6433       the publication should not be replicated,
6434       <literal>s</literal> = stored generated columns in the tables associated
6435       with the publication should be replicated.
6436      </para></entry>
6437     </row>
6438    </tbody>
6439   </tgroup>
6440  </table>
6441 </sect1>
6442
6443 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-publication-namespace">
6444  <title><structname>pg_publication_namespace</structname></title>
6445
6446  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-publication-namespace">
6447   <primary>pg_publication_namespace</primary>
6448  </indexterm>
6449
6450  <para>
6451   The catalog <structname>pg_publication_namespace</structname> contains the
6452   mapping between schemas and publications in the database.  This is a
6453   many-to-many mapping.
6454  </para>
6455
6456  <table>
6457   <title><structname>pg_publication_namespace</structname> Columns</title>
6458   <tgroup cols="1">
6459    <thead>
6460     <row>
6461      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6462       Column Type
6463      </para>
6464      <para>
6465       Description
6466      </para></entry>
6467     </row>
6468    </thead>
6469
6470    <tbody>
6471     <row>
6472      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6473       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6474      </para>
6475      <para>
6476       Row identifier
6477      </para></entry>
6478     </row>
6479
6480     <row>
6481      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6482       <structfield>pnpubid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6483       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-publication"><structname>pg_publication</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6484      </para>
6485      <para>
6486       Reference to publication
6487      </para></entry>
6488     </row>
6489
6490     <row>
6491      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6492       <structfield>pnnspid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6493       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6494      </para>
6495      <para>
6496       Reference to schema
6497      </para></entry>
6498     </row>
6499    </tbody>
6500   </tgroup>
6501  </table>
6502 </sect1>
6503
6504 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-publication-rel">
6505  <title><structname>pg_publication_rel</structname></title>
6506
6507  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-publication-rel">
6508   <primary>pg_publication_rel</primary>
6509  </indexterm>
6510
6511  <para>
6512   The catalog <structname>pg_publication_rel</structname> contains the
6513   mapping between relations and publications in the database.  This is a
6514   many-to-many mapping.  See also <xref linkend="view-pg-publication-tables"/>
6515   for a more user-friendly view of this information.
6516  </para>
6517
6518  <table>
6519   <title><structname>pg_publication_rel</structname> Columns</title>
6520   <tgroup cols="1">
6521    <thead>
6522     <row>
6523      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6524       Column Type
6525      </para>
6526      <para>
6527       Description
6528      </para></entry>
6529     </row>
6530    </thead>
6531
6532    <tbody>
6533     <row>
6534      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6535       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6536      </para>
6537      <para>
6538       Row identifier
6539      </para></entry>
6540     </row>
6541
6542     <row>
6543      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6544       <structfield>prpubid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6545       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-publication"><structname>pg_publication</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6546      </para>
6547      <para>
6548       Reference to publication
6549      </para></entry>
6550     </row>
6551
6552     <row>
6553      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6554       <structfield>prrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6555       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6556      </para>
6557      <para>
6558       Reference to relation
6559      </para></entry>
6560     </row>
6561
6562     <row>
6563      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6564      <structfield>prqual</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
6565      </para>
6566      <para>Expression tree (in <function>nodeToString()</function>
6567      representation) for the relation's publication qualifying condition. Null
6568      if there is no publication qualifying condition.</para></entry>
6569     </row>
6570
6571     <row>
6572      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6573       <structfield>prattrs</structfield> <type>int2vector</type>
6574       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link>.<structfield>attnum</structfield>)
6575      </para>
6576      <para>
6577       This is an array of values that indicates which table columns are
6578       part of the publication.  For example, a value of <literal>1 3</literal>
6579       would mean that the first and the third table columns are published.
6580       A null value indicates that all columns are published.
6581      </para></entry>
6582     </row>
6583    </tbody>
6584   </tgroup>
6585  </table>
6586 </sect1>
6587
6588 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-range">
6589  <title><structname>pg_range</structname></title>
6590
6591  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-range">
6592   <primary>pg_range</primary>
6593  </indexterm>
6594
6595  <para>
6596   The catalog <structname>pg_range</structname> stores information about
6597   range types.  This is in addition to the types' entries in
6598   <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.
6599  </para>
6600
6601  <table>
6602   <title><structname>pg_range</structname> Columns</title>
6603   <tgroup cols="1">
6604    <thead>
6605     <row>
6606      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6607       Column Type
6608      </para>
6609      <para>
6610       Description
6611      </para></entry>
6612     </row>
6613    </thead>
6614
6615    <tbody>
6616     <row>
6617      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6618       <structfield>rngtypid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6619       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6620      </para>
6621      <para>
6622       OID of the range type
6623      </para></entry>
6624     </row>
6625
6626     <row>
6627      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6628       <structfield>rngsubtype</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6629       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6630      </para>
6631      <para>
6632       OID of the element type (subtype) of this range type
6633      </para></entry>
6634     </row>
6635
6636     <row>
6637      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6638       <structfield>rngmultitypid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6639       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6640      </para>
6641      <para>
6642       OID of the multirange type for this range type
6643      </para></entry>
6644     </row>
6645
6646     <row>
6647      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6648       <structfield>rngcollation</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6649       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-collation"><structname>pg_collation</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6650      </para>
6651      <para>
6652       OID of the collation used for range comparisons, or zero if none
6653      </para></entry>
6654     </row>
6655
6656     <row>
6657      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6658       <structfield>rngsubopc</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6659       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-opclass"><structname>pg_opclass</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6660      </para>
6661      <para>
6662       OID of the subtype's operator class used for range comparisons
6663      </para></entry>
6664     </row>
6665
6666     <row>
6667      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6668       <structfield>rngcanonical</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
6669       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6670      </para>
6671      <para>
6672       OID of the function to convert a range value into canonical form,
6673       or zero if none
6674      </para></entry>
6675     </row>
6676
6677     <row>
6678      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6679       <structfield>rngsubdiff</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
6680       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6681      </para>
6682      <para>
6683       OID of the function to return the difference between two element
6684       values as <type>double precision</type>, or zero if none
6685      </para></entry>
6686     </row>
6687    </tbody>
6688   </tgroup>
6689  </table>
6690
6691  <para>
6692   <structfield>rngsubopc</structfield> (plus <structfield>rngcollation</structfield>, if the
6693   element type is collatable) determines the sort ordering used by the range
6694   type.  <structfield>rngcanonical</structfield> is used when the element type is
6695   discrete.  <structfield>rngsubdiff</structfield> is optional but should be supplied to
6696   improve performance of GiST indexes on the range type.
6697  </para>
6698
6699 </sect1>
6700
6701 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-replication-origin">
6702  <title><structname>pg_replication_origin</structname></title>
6703
6704  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-replication-origin">
6705   <primary>pg_replication_origin</primary>
6706  </indexterm>
6707
6708  <para>
6709   The <structname>pg_replication_origin</structname> catalog contains
6710   all replication origins created.  For more on replication origins
6711   see <xref linkend="replication-origins"/>.
6712  </para>
6713
6714  <para>
6715   Unlike most system catalogs, <structname>pg_replication_origin</structname>
6716   is shared across all databases of a cluster: there is only one copy
6717   of <structname>pg_replication_origin</structname> per cluster, not one per
6718   database.
6719  </para>
6720
6721  <table>
6722   <title><structname>pg_replication_origin</structname> Columns</title>
6723   <tgroup cols="1">
6724    <thead>
6725     <row>
6726      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6727       Column Type
6728      </para>
6729      <para>
6730       Description
6731      </para></entry>
6732     </row>
6733    </thead>
6734
6735    <tbody>
6736     <row>
6737      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6738       <structfield>roident</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6739      </para>
6740      <para>
6741       A unique, cluster-wide identifier for the replication
6742       origin. Should never leave the system.
6743      </para></entry>
6744     </row>
6745
6746     <row>
6747      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6748       <structfield>roname</structfield> <type>text</type>
6749      </para>
6750      <para>
6751       The external, user defined, name of a replication
6752       origin.
6753      </para></entry>
6754     </row>
6755    </tbody>
6756   </tgroup>
6757  </table>
6758 </sect1>
6759
6760 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-rewrite">
6761  <title><structname>pg_rewrite</structname></title>
6762
6763  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-rewrite">
6764   <primary>pg_rewrite</primary>
6765  </indexterm>
6766
6767  <para>
6768   The catalog <structname>pg_rewrite</structname> stores rewrite rules for tables and views.
6769  </para>
6770
6771  <table>
6772   <title><structname>pg_rewrite</structname> Columns</title>
6773   <tgroup cols="1">
6774    <thead>
6775     <row>
6776      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6777       Column Type
6778      </para>
6779      <para>
6780       Description
6781      </para></entry>
6782     </row>
6783    </thead>
6784
6785    <tbody>
6786     <row>
6787      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6788       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6789      </para>
6790      <para>
6791       Row identifier
6792      </para></entry>
6793     </row>
6794
6795     <row>
6796      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6797       <structfield>rulename</structfield> <type>name</type>
6798      </para>
6799      <para>
6800       Rule name
6801      </para></entry>
6802     </row>
6803
6804     <row>
6805      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6806       <structfield>ev_class</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6807       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6808      </para>
6809      <para>
6810       The table this rule is for
6811      </para></entry>
6812     </row>
6813
6814     <row>
6815      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6816       <structfield>ev_type</structfield> <type>char</type>
6817      </para>
6818      <para>
6819       Event type that the rule is for: 1 = <xref linkend="sql-select"/>, 2 =
6820       <xref linkend="sql-update"/>, 3 = <xref linkend="sql-insert"/>, 4 =
6821       <xref linkend="sql-delete"/>
6822      </para></entry>
6823     </row>
6824
6825     <row>
6826      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6827       <structfield>ev_enabled</structfield> <type>char</type>
6828      </para>
6829      <para>
6830       Controls in which <xref linkend="guc-session-replication-role"/> modes
6831       the rule fires.
6832       <literal>O</literal> = rule fires in <quote>origin</quote> and <quote>local</quote> modes,
6833       <literal>D</literal> = rule is disabled,
6834       <literal>R</literal> = rule fires in <quote>replica</quote> mode,
6835       <literal>A</literal> = rule fires always.
6836      </para></entry>
6837     </row>
6838
6839     <row>
6840      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6841       <structfield>is_instead</structfield> <type>bool</type>
6842      </para>
6843      <para>
6844       True if the rule is an <literal>INSTEAD</literal> rule
6845      </para></entry>
6846     </row>
6847
6848     <row>
6849      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6850       <structfield>ev_qual</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
6851      </para>
6852      <para>
6853       Expression tree (in the form of a
6854       <function>nodeToString()</function> representation) for the
6855       rule's qualifying condition
6856      </para></entry>
6857     </row>
6858
6859     <row>
6860      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6861       <structfield>ev_action</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
6862      </para>
6863      <para>
6864       Query tree (in the form of a
6865       <function>nodeToString()</function> representation) for the
6866       rule's action
6867      </para></entry>
6868     </row>
6869    </tbody>
6870   </tgroup>
6871  </table>
6872
6873  <note>
6874   <para>
6875    <literal>pg_class.relhasrules</literal>
6876    must be true if a table has any rules in this catalog.
6877   </para>
6878  </note>
6879
6880 </sect1>
6881
6882 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-seclabel">
6883  <title><structname>pg_seclabel</structname></title>
6884
6885  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-seclabel">
6886   <primary>pg_seclabel</primary>
6887  </indexterm>
6888
6889  <para>
6890   The catalog <structname>pg_seclabel</structname> stores security
6891   labels on database objects.  Security labels can be manipulated
6892   with the <link linkend="sql-security-label"><command>SECURITY LABEL</command></link> command.  For an easier
6893   way to view security labels, see <xref linkend="view-pg-seclabels"/>.
6894  </para>
6895
6896  <para>
6897   See also <link linkend="catalog-pg-shseclabel"><structname>pg_shseclabel</structname></link>,
6898   which performs a similar function for security labels of database objects
6899   that are shared across a database cluster.
6900  </para>
6901
6902  <table>
6903   <title><structname>pg_seclabel</structname> Columns</title>
6904   <tgroup cols="1">
6905    <thead>
6906     <row>
6907      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6908       Column Type
6909      </para>
6910      <para>
6911       Description
6912      </para></entry>
6913     </row>
6914    </thead>
6915
6916    <tbody>
6917     <row>
6918      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6919       <structfield>objoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6920       (references any OID column)
6921      </para>
6922      <para>
6923       The OID of the object this security label pertains to
6924      </para></entry>
6925     </row>
6926
6927     <row>
6928      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6929       <structfield>classoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
6930       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
6931      </para>
6932      <para>
6933       The OID of the system catalog this object appears in
6934      </para></entry>
6935     </row>
6936
6937     <row>
6938      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6939       <structfield>objsubid</structfield> <type>int4</type>
6940      </para>
6941      <para>
6942       For a security label on a table column, this is the column number (the
6943       <structfield>objoid</structfield> and <structfield>classoid</structfield> refer to
6944       the table itself).  For all other object types, this column is
6945       zero.
6946      </para></entry>
6947     </row>
6948
6949     <row>
6950      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6951       <structfield>provider</structfield> <type>text</type>
6952      </para>
6953      <para>
6954       The label provider associated with this label.
6955      </para></entry>
6956     </row>
6957
6958     <row>
6959      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6960       <structfield>label</structfield> <type>text</type>
6961      </para>
6962      <para>
6963       The security label applied to this object.
6964      </para></entry>
6965     </row>
6966    </tbody>
6967   </tgroup>
6968  </table>
6969 </sect1>
6970
6971 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-sequence">
6972  <title><structname>pg_sequence</structname></title>
6973
6974  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-sequence">
6975   <primary>pg_sequence</primary>
6976  </indexterm>
6977
6978  <para>
6979   The catalog <structname>pg_sequence</structname> contains information about
6980   sequences.  Some of the information about sequences, such as the name and
6981   the schema, is in
6982   <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>
6983  </para>
6984
6985  <table>
6986   <title><structname>pg_sequence</structname> Columns</title>
6987   <tgroup cols="1">
6988    <thead>
6989     <row>
6990      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
6991       Column Type
6992      </para>
6993      <para>
6994       Description
6995      </para></entry>
6996     </row>
6997    </thead>
6998
6999    <tbody>
7000     <row>
7001      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7002       <structfield>seqrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7003       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7004      </para>
7005      <para>
7006       The OID of the <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link> entry for this sequence
7007      </para></entry>
7008     </row>
7009
7010     <row>
7011      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7012       <structfield>seqtypid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7013       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7014      </para>
7015      <para>
7016       Data type of the sequence
7017      </para></entry>
7018     </row>
7019
7020     <row>
7021      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7022       <structfield>seqstart</structfield> <type>int8</type>
7023      </para>
7024      <para>
7025       Start value of the sequence
7026      </para></entry>
7027     </row>
7028
7029     <row>
7030      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7031       <structfield>seqincrement</structfield> <type>int8</type>
7032      </para>
7033      <para>
7034       Increment value of the sequence
7035      </para></entry>
7036     </row>
7037
7038     <row>
7039      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7040       <structfield>seqmax</structfield> <type>int8</type>
7041      </para>
7042      <para>
7043       Maximum value of the sequence
7044      </para></entry>
7045     </row>
7046
7047     <row>
7048      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7049       <structfield>seqmin</structfield> <type>int8</type>
7050      </para>
7051      <para>
7052       Minimum value of the sequence
7053      </para></entry>
7054     </row>
7055
7056     <row>
7057      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7058       <structfield>seqcache</structfield> <type>int8</type>
7059      </para>
7060      <para>
7061       Cache size of the sequence
7062      </para></entry>
7063     </row>
7064
7065     <row>
7066      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7067       <structfield>seqcycle</structfield> <type>bool</type>
7068      </para>
7069      <para>
7070       Whether the sequence cycles
7071      </para></entry>
7072     </row>
7073    </tbody>
7074   </tgroup>
7075  </table>
7076 </sect1>
7077
7078 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-shdepend">
7079  <title><structname>pg_shdepend</structname></title>
7080
7081  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-shdepend">
7082   <primary>pg_shdepend</primary>
7083  </indexterm>
7084
7085  <para>
7086   The catalog <structname>pg_shdepend</structname> records the
7087   dependency relationships between database objects and shared objects,
7088   such as roles.  This information allows
7089   <productname>PostgreSQL</productname> to ensure that those objects are
7090   unreferenced before attempting to delete them.
7091  </para>
7092
7093  <para>
7094   See also <link linkend="catalog-pg-depend"><structname>pg_depend</structname></link>,
7095   which performs a similar function for dependencies involving objects
7096   within a single database.
7097  </para>
7098
7099  <para>
7100   Unlike most system catalogs, <structname>pg_shdepend</structname>
7101   is shared across all databases of a cluster: there is only one
7102   copy of <structname>pg_shdepend</structname> per cluster, not
7103   one per database.
7104  </para>
7105
7106  <table>
7107   <title><structname>pg_shdepend</structname> Columns</title>
7108   <tgroup cols="1">
7109    <thead>
7110     <row>
7111      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7112       Column Type
7113      </para>
7114      <para>
7115       Description
7116      </para></entry>
7117     </row>
7118    </thead>
7119
7120    <tbody>
7121     <row>
7122      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7123       <structfield>dbid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7124       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-database"><structname>pg_database</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7125      </para>
7126      <para>
7127       The OID of the database the dependent object is in,
7128       or zero for a shared object
7129      </para></entry>
7130     </row>
7131
7132     <row>
7133      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7134       <structfield>classid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7135       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7136      </para>
7137      <para>
7138       The OID of the system catalog the dependent object is in
7139      </para></entry>
7140     </row>
7141
7142     <row>
7143      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7144       <structfield>objid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7145       (references any OID column)
7146      </para>
7147      <para>
7148       The OID of the specific dependent object
7149      </para></entry>
7150     </row>
7151
7152     <row>
7153      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7154       <structfield>objsubid</structfield> <type>int4</type>
7155      </para>
7156      <para>
7157       For a table column, this is the column number (the
7158       <structfield>objid</structfield> and <structfield>classid</structfield> refer to the
7159       table itself).  For all other object types, this column is zero.
7160      </para></entry>
7161     </row>
7162
7163     <row>
7164      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7165       <structfield>refclassid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7166       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7167      </para>
7168      <para>
7169       The OID of the system catalog the referenced object is in
7170       (must be a shared catalog)
7171      </para></entry>
7172     </row>
7173
7174     <row>
7175      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7176       <structfield>refobjid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7177       (references any OID column)
7178      </para>
7179      <para>
7180       The OID of the specific referenced object
7181      </para></entry>
7182     </row>
7183
7184     <row>
7185      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7186       <structfield>deptype</structfield> <type>char</type>
7187      </para>
7188      <para>
7189       A code defining the specific semantics of this dependency relationship; see text
7190      </para></entry>
7191     </row>
7192    </tbody>
7193   </tgroup>
7194  </table>
7195
7196  <para>
7197   In all cases, a <structname>pg_shdepend</structname> entry indicates that
7198   the referenced object cannot be dropped without also dropping the dependent
7199   object.  However, there are several subflavors identified by
7200   <structfield>deptype</structfield>:
7201
7202   <variablelist>
7203    <varlistentry>
7204     <term><symbol>SHARED_DEPENDENCY_OWNER</symbol> (<literal>o</literal>)</term>
7205     <listitem>
7206      <para>
7207       The referenced object (which must be a role) is the owner of the
7208       dependent object.
7209      </para>
7210     </listitem>
7211    </varlistentry>
7212
7213    <varlistentry>
7214     <term><symbol>SHARED_DEPENDENCY_ACL</symbol> (<literal>a</literal>)</term>
7215     <listitem>
7216      <para>
7217       The referenced object (which must be a role) is mentioned in the
7218       <acronym>ACL</acronym> of the
7219       dependent object.  (A <symbol>SHARED_DEPENDENCY_ACL</symbol> entry is
7220       not made for the owner of the object, since the owner will have
7221       a <symbol>SHARED_DEPENDENCY_OWNER</symbol> entry anyway.)
7222      </para>
7223     </listitem>
7224    </varlistentry>
7225
7226    <varlistentry>
7227     <term><symbol>SHARED_DEPENDENCY_INITACL</symbol> (<literal>i</literal>)</term>
7228     <listitem>
7229      <para>
7230       The referenced object (which must be a role) is mentioned in a
7231       <link linkend="catalog-pg-init-privs"><structname>pg_init_privs</structname></link>
7232       entry for the dependent object.
7233      </para>
7234     </listitem>
7235    </varlistentry>
7236
7237    <varlistentry>
7238     <term><symbol>SHARED_DEPENDENCY_POLICY</symbol> (<literal>r</literal>)</term>
7239     <listitem>
7240      <para>
7241       The referenced object (which must be a role) is mentioned as the
7242       target of a dependent policy object.
7243      </para>
7244     </listitem>
7245    </varlistentry>
7246
7247    <varlistentry>
7248     <term><symbol>SHARED_DEPENDENCY_TABLESPACE</symbol> (<literal>t</literal>)</term>
7249     <listitem>
7250      <para>
7251       The referenced object (which must be a tablespace) is mentioned as
7252       the tablespace for a relation that doesn't have storage.
7253      </para>
7254     </listitem>
7255    </varlistentry>
7256   </variablelist>
7257
7258   Other dependency flavors might be needed in future.  Note in particular
7259   that the current definition only supports roles and tablespaces as referenced
7260   objects.
7261  </para>
7262
7263  <para>
7264   As in the <structname>pg_depend</structname> catalog, most objects
7265   created during <application>initdb</application> are
7266   considered <quote>pinned</quote>.  No entries are made
7267   in <structname>pg_shdepend</structname> that would have a pinned
7268   object as either referenced or dependent object.
7269  </para>
7270
7271 </sect1>
7272
7273 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-shdescription">
7274  <title><structname>pg_shdescription</structname></title>
7275
7276  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-shdescription">
7277   <primary>pg_shdescription</primary>
7278  </indexterm>
7279
7280  <para>
7281   The catalog <structname>pg_shdescription</structname> stores optional
7282   descriptions (comments) for shared database objects.  Descriptions can be
7283   manipulated with the <link linkend="sql-comment"><command>COMMENT</command></link> command and viewed with
7284   <application>psql</application>'s <literal>\d</literal> commands.
7285  </para>
7286
7287  <para>
7288   See also <link linkend="catalog-pg-description"><structname>pg_description</structname></link>,
7289   which performs a similar function for descriptions involving objects
7290   within a single database.
7291  </para>
7292
7293  <para>
7294   Unlike most system catalogs, <structname>pg_shdescription</structname>
7295   is shared across all databases of a cluster: there is only one
7296   copy of <structname>pg_shdescription</structname> per cluster, not
7297   one per database.
7298  </para>
7299
7300  <table>
7301   <title><structname>pg_shdescription</structname> Columns</title>
7302   <tgroup cols="1">
7303    <thead>
7304     <row>
7305      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7306       Column Type
7307      </para>
7308      <para>
7309       Description
7310      </para></entry>
7311     </row>
7312    </thead>
7313
7314    <tbody>
7315     <row>
7316      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7317       <structfield>objoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7318       (references any OID column)
7319      </para>
7320      <para>
7321       The OID of the object this description pertains to
7322      </para></entry>
7323     </row>
7324
7325     <row>
7326      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7327       <structfield>classoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7328       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7329      </para>
7330      <para>
7331       The OID of the system catalog this object appears in
7332      </para></entry>
7333     </row>
7334
7335     <row>
7336      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7337       <structfield>description</structfield> <type>text</type>
7338      </para>
7339      <para>
7340       Arbitrary text that serves as the description of this object
7341      </para></entry>
7342     </row>
7343    </tbody>
7344   </tgroup>
7345  </table>
7346
7347 </sect1>
7348
7349 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-shseclabel">
7350  <title><structname>pg_shseclabel</structname></title>
7351
7352  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-shseclabel">
7353   <primary>pg_shseclabel</primary>
7354  </indexterm>
7355
7356  <para>
7357   The catalog <structname>pg_shseclabel</structname> stores security
7358   labels on shared database objects.  Security labels can be manipulated
7359   with the <link linkend="sql-security-label"><command>SECURITY LABEL</command></link> command.  For an easier
7360   way to view security labels, see <xref linkend="view-pg-seclabels"/>.
7361  </para>
7362
7363  <para>
7364   See also <link linkend="catalog-pg-seclabel"><structname>pg_seclabel</structname></link>,
7365   which performs a similar function for security labels involving objects
7366   within a single database.
7367  </para>
7368
7369  <para>
7370   Unlike most system catalogs, <structname>pg_shseclabel</structname>
7371   is shared across all databases of a cluster: there is only one
7372   copy of <structname>pg_shseclabel</structname> per cluster, not
7373   one per database.
7374  </para>
7375
7376  <table>
7377   <title><structname>pg_shseclabel</structname> Columns</title>
7378   <tgroup cols="1">
7379    <thead>
7380     <row>
7381      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7382       Column Type
7383      </para>
7384      <para>
7385       Description
7386      </para></entry>
7387     </row>
7388    </thead>
7389
7390    <tbody>
7391     <row>
7392      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7393       <structfield>objoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7394       (references any OID column)
7395      </para>
7396      <para>
7397       The OID of the object this security label pertains to
7398      </para></entry>
7399     </row>
7400
7401     <row>
7402      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7403       <structfield>classoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7404       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7405      </para>
7406      <para>
7407       The OID of the system catalog this object appears in
7408      </para></entry>
7409     </row>
7410
7411     <row>
7412      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7413       <structfield>provider</structfield> <type>text</type>
7414      </para>
7415      <para>
7416       The label provider associated with this label.
7417      </para></entry>
7418     </row>
7419
7420     <row>
7421      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7422       <structfield>label</structfield> <type>text</type>
7423      </para>
7424      <para>
7425       The security label applied to this object.
7426      </para></entry>
7427     </row>
7428    </tbody>
7429   </tgroup>
7430  </table>
7431 </sect1>
7432
7433 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-statistic">
7434  <title><structname>pg_statistic</structname></title>
7435
7436  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-statistic">
7437   <primary>pg_statistic</primary>
7438  </indexterm>
7439
7440  <para>
7441   The catalog <structname>pg_statistic</structname> stores
7442   statistical data about the contents of the database.  Entries are
7443   created by <link linkend="sql-analyze"><command>ANALYZE</command></link>
7444   and subsequently used by the query planner.  Note that all the
7445   statistical data is inherently approximate, even assuming that it
7446   is up-to-date.
7447  </para>
7448
7449  <para>
7450   Normally there is one entry, with <structfield>stainherit</structfield> =
7451   <literal>false</literal>, for each table column that has been analyzed.
7452   If the table has inheritance children or partitions, a second entry with
7453   <structfield>stainherit</structfield> = <literal>true</literal> is also created.  This row
7454   represents the column's statistics over the inheritance tree, i.e.,
7455   statistics for the data you'd see with
7456   <literal>SELECT <replaceable>column</replaceable> FROM <replaceable>table</replaceable>*</literal>,
7457   whereas the <structfield>stainherit</structfield> = <literal>false</literal> row represents
7458   the results of
7459   <literal>SELECT <replaceable>column</replaceable> FROM ONLY <replaceable>table</replaceable></literal>.
7460  </para>
7461
7462  <para>
7463   <structname>pg_statistic</structname> also stores statistical data about
7464   the values of index expressions.  These are described as if they were
7465   actual data columns; in particular, <structfield>starelid</structfield>
7466   references the index.  No entry is made for an ordinary non-expression
7467   index column, however, since it would be redundant with the entry
7468   for the underlying table column.  Currently, entries for index expressions
7469   always have <structfield>stainherit</structfield> = <literal>false</literal>.
7470  </para>
7471
7472  <para>
7473   Since different kinds of statistics might be appropriate for different
7474   kinds of data, <structname>pg_statistic</structname> is designed not
7475   to assume very much about what sort of statistics it stores.  Only
7476   extremely general statistics (such as nullness) are given dedicated
7477   columns in <structname>pg_statistic</structname>.  Everything else
7478   is stored in <quote>slots</quote>, which are groups of associated columns
7479   whose content is identified by a code number in one of the slot's columns.
7480   For more information see
7481   <filename>src/include/catalog/pg_statistic.h</filename>.
7482  </para>
7483
7484  <para>
7485   <structname>pg_statistic</structname> should not be readable by the
7486   public, since even statistical information about a table's contents
7487   might be considered sensitive.  (Example: minimum and maximum values
7488   of a salary column might be quite interesting.)
7489   <link linkend="view-pg-stats"><structname>pg_stats</structname></link>
7490   is a publicly readable view on
7491   <structname>pg_statistic</structname> that only exposes information
7492   about those tables that are readable by the current user.
7493  </para>
7494
7495  <table>
7496   <title><structname>pg_statistic</structname> Columns</title>
7497   <tgroup cols="1">
7498    <thead>
7499     <row>
7500      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7501       Column Type
7502      </para>
7503      <para>
7504       Description
7505      </para></entry>
7506     </row>
7507    </thead>
7508
7509    <tbody>
7510     <row>
7511      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7512       <structfield>starelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7513       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7514      </para>
7515      <para>
7516       The table or index that the described column belongs to
7517      </para></entry>
7518     </row>
7519
7520     <row>
7521      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7522       <structfield>staattnum</structfield> <type>int2</type>
7523       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link>.<structfield>attnum</structfield>)
7524      </para>
7525      <para>
7526       The number of the described column
7527      </para></entry>
7528     </row>
7529
7530     <row>
7531      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7532       <structfield>stainherit</structfield> <type>bool</type>
7533      </para>
7534      <para>
7535       If true, the stats include values from child tables, not just the
7536       values in the specified relation
7537      </para></entry>
7538     </row>
7539
7540     <row>
7541      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7542       <structfield>stanullfrac</structfield> <type>float4</type>
7543      </para>
7544      <para>
7545       The fraction of the column's entries that are null
7546      </para></entry>
7547     </row>
7548
7549     <row>
7550      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7551       <structfield>stawidth</structfield> <type>int4</type>
7552      </para>
7553      <para>
7554       The average stored width, in bytes, of nonnull entries
7555      </para></entry>
7556     </row>
7557
7558     <row>
7559      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7560       <structfield>stadistinct</structfield> <type>float4</type>
7561      </para>
7562      <para>
7563       The number of distinct nonnull data values in the column.
7564       A value greater than zero is the actual number of distinct values.
7565       A value less than zero is the negative of a multiplier for the number
7566       of rows in the table; for example, a column in which about 80% of the
7567       values are nonnull and each nonnull value appears about twice on
7568       average could be represented by <structfield>stadistinct</structfield> = -0.4.
7569       A zero value means the number of distinct values is unknown.
7570      </para></entry>
7571     </row>
7572
7573     <row>
7574      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7575       <structfield>stakind<replaceable>N</replaceable></structfield> <type>int2</type>
7576      </para>
7577      <para>
7578       A code number indicating the kind of statistics stored in the
7579       <replaceable>N</replaceable>th <quote>slot</quote> of the
7580       <structname>pg_statistic</structname> row.
7581      </para></entry>
7582     </row>
7583
7584     <row>
7585      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7586       <structfield>staop<replaceable>N</replaceable></structfield> <type>oid</type>
7587       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-operator"><structname>pg_operator</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7588      </para>
7589      <para>
7590       An operator used to derive the statistics stored in the
7591       <replaceable>N</replaceable>th <quote>slot</quote>.  For example, a
7592       histogram slot would show the <literal>&lt;</literal> operator
7593       that defines the sort order of the data.
7594       Zero if the statistics kind does not require an operator.
7595      </para></entry>
7596     </row>
7597
7598     <row>
7599      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7600       <structfield>stacoll<replaceable>N</replaceable></structfield> <type>oid</type>
7601       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-collation"><structname>pg_collation</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7602      </para>
7603      <para>
7604       The collation used to derive the statistics stored in the
7605       <replaceable>N</replaceable>th <quote>slot</quote>.  For example, a
7606       histogram slot for a collatable column would show the collation that
7607       defines the sort order of the data.  Zero for noncollatable data.
7608      </para></entry>
7609     </row>
7610
7611     <row>
7612      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7613       <structfield>stanumbers<replaceable>N</replaceable></structfield> <type>float4[]</type>
7614      </para>
7615      <para>
7616       Numerical statistics of the appropriate kind for the
7617       <replaceable>N</replaceable>th <quote>slot</quote>, or null if the slot
7618       kind does not involve numerical values
7619      </para></entry>
7620     </row>
7621
7622     <row>
7623      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7624       <structfield>stavalues<replaceable>N</replaceable></structfield> <type>anyarray</type>
7625      </para>
7626      <para>
7627       Column data values of the appropriate kind for the
7628       <replaceable>N</replaceable>th <quote>slot</quote>, or null if the slot
7629       kind does not store any data values.  Each array's element
7630       values are actually of the specific column's data type, or a related
7631       type such as an array's element type, so there is no way to define
7632       these columns' type more specifically than <type>anyarray</type>.
7633      </para></entry>
7634     </row>
7635    </tbody>
7636   </tgroup>
7637  </table>
7638
7639 </sect1>
7640
7641 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-statistic-ext">
7642  <title><structname>pg_statistic_ext</structname></title>
7643
7644  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-statistic-ext">
7645   <primary>pg_statistic_ext</primary>
7646  </indexterm>
7647
7648  <para>
7649   The catalog <structname>pg_statistic_ext</structname>
7650   holds definitions of extended planner statistics.
7651   Each row in this catalog corresponds to a <firstterm>statistics object</firstterm>
7652   created with <link linkend="sql-createstatistics"><command>CREATE STATISTICS</command></link>.
7653  </para>
7654
7655  <table>
7656   <title><structname>pg_statistic_ext</structname> Columns</title>
7657   <tgroup cols="1">
7658    <thead>
7659     <row>
7660      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7661       Column Type
7662      </para>
7663      <para>
7664       Description
7665      </para></entry>
7666     </row>
7667    </thead>
7668
7669    <tbody>
7670     <row>
7671      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7672       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7673      </para>
7674      <para>
7675       Row identifier
7676      </para></entry>
7677     </row>
7678
7679     <row>
7680      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7681       <structfield>stxrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7682       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7683      </para>
7684      <para>
7685       Table containing the columns described by this object
7686      </para></entry>
7687     </row>
7688
7689     <row>
7690      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7691       <structfield>stxname</structfield> <type>name</type>
7692      </para>
7693      <para>
7694       Name of the statistics object
7695      </para></entry>
7696     </row>
7697
7698     <row>
7699      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7700       <structfield>stxnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7701       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7702      </para>
7703      <para>
7704       The OID of the namespace that contains this statistics object
7705      </para></entry>
7706     </row>
7707
7708     <row>
7709      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7710       <structfield>stxowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7711       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7712      </para>
7713      <para>
7714       Owner of the statistics object
7715      </para></entry>
7716     </row>
7717
7718     <row>
7719      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7720       <structfield>stxkeys</structfield> <type>int2vector</type>
7721       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link>.<structfield>attnum</structfield>)
7722      </para>
7723      <para>
7724       An array of attribute numbers, indicating which table columns are
7725       covered by this statistics object;
7726       for example a value of <literal>1 3</literal> would
7727       mean that the first and the third table columns are covered
7728      </para></entry>
7729     </row>
7730
7731     <row>
7732      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7733       <structfield>stxstattarget</structfield> <type>int2</type>
7734      </para>
7735      <para>
7736       <structfield>stxstattarget</structfield> controls the level of detail
7737       of statistics accumulated for this statistics object by
7738       <link linkend="sql-analyze"><command>ANALYZE</command></link>.
7739       A zero value indicates that no statistics should be collected.
7740       A null value says to use the maximum of the statistics targets of
7741       the referenced columns, if set, or the system default statistics target.
7742       Positive values of <structfield>stxstattarget</structfield>
7743       determine the target number of <quote>most common values</quote>
7744       to collect.
7745      </para></entry>
7746     </row>
7747
7748     <row>
7749      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7750       <structfield>stxkind</structfield> <type>char[]</type>
7751      </para>
7752      <para>
7753       An array containing codes for the enabled statistics kinds;
7754       valid values are:
7755       <literal>d</literal> for n-distinct statistics,
7756       <literal>f</literal> for functional dependency statistics,
7757       <literal>m</literal> for most common values (MCV) list statistics, and
7758       <literal>e</literal> for expression statistics
7759      </para></entry>
7760     </row>
7761
7762     <row>
7763      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7764       <structfield>stxexprs</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
7765      </para>
7766      <para>
7767       Expression trees (in <function>nodeToString()</function>
7768       representation) for statistics object attributes that are not simple
7769       column references.  This is a list with one element per expression.
7770       Null if all statistics object attributes are simple references.
7771      </para></entry>
7772     </row>
7773
7774    </tbody>
7775   </tgroup>
7776  </table>
7777
7778  <para>
7779   The <structname>pg_statistic_ext</structname> entry is filled in
7780   completely during <link linkend="sql-createstatistics"><command>CREATE STATISTICS</command></link>, but the actual
7781   statistical values are not computed then.
7782   Subsequent <link linkend="sql-analyze"><command>ANALYZE</command></link> commands compute the desired values
7783   and populate an entry in the
7784   <link linkend="catalog-pg-statistic-ext-data"><structname>pg_statistic_ext_data</structname></link>
7785   catalog.
7786  </para>
7787 </sect1>
7788
7789 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-statistic-ext-data">
7790  <title><structname>pg_statistic_ext_data</structname></title>
7791
7792  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-statistic-ext">
7793   <primary>pg_statistic_ext_data</primary>
7794  </indexterm>
7795
7796  <para>
7797   The catalog <structname>pg_statistic_ext_data</structname>
7798   holds data for extended planner statistics defined in
7799   <link linkend="catalog-pg-statistic-ext"><structname>pg_statistic_ext</structname></link>.
7800   Each row in this catalog corresponds to a <firstterm>statistics object</firstterm>
7801   created with <link linkend="sql-createstatistics"><command>CREATE STATISTICS</command></link>.
7802  </para>
7803
7804  <para>
7805   Normally there is one entry, with <structfield>stxdinherit</structfield> =
7806   <literal>false</literal>, for each statistics object that has been analyzed.
7807   If the table has inheritance children or partitions, a second entry with
7808   <structfield>stxdinherit</structfield> = <literal>true</literal> is also created.
7809   This row represents the statistics object over the inheritance tree, i.e.,
7810   statistics for the data you'd see with
7811   <literal>SELECT * FROM <replaceable>table</replaceable>*</literal>,
7812   whereas the <structfield>stxdinherit</structfield> = <literal>false</literal> row
7813   represents the results of
7814   <literal>SELECT * FROM ONLY <replaceable>table</replaceable></literal>.
7815  </para>
7816
7817  <para>
7818   Like <link linkend="catalog-pg-statistic"><structname>pg_statistic</structname></link>,
7819   <structname>pg_statistic_ext_data</structname> should not be
7820   readable by the public, since the contents might be considered sensitive.
7821   (Example: most common combinations of values in columns might be quite
7822   interesting.)
7823   <link linkend="view-pg-stats-ext"><structname>pg_stats_ext</structname></link>
7824   is a publicly readable view
7825   on <structname>pg_statistic_ext_data</structname> (after joining
7826   with <link linkend="catalog-pg-statistic-ext"><structname>pg_statistic_ext</structname></link>) that only exposes
7827   information about tables the current user owns.
7828  </para>
7829
7830  <table>
7831   <title><structname>pg_statistic_ext_data</structname> Columns</title>
7832   <tgroup cols="1">
7833    <thead>
7834     <row>
7835      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7836       Column Type
7837      </para>
7838      <para>
7839       Description
7840      </para></entry>
7841     </row>
7842    </thead>
7843
7844    <tbody>
7845     <row>
7846      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7847       <structfield>stxoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7848       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-statistic-ext"><structname>pg_statistic_ext</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7849      </para>
7850      <para>
7851       Extended statistics object containing the definition for this data
7852      </para></entry>
7853     </row>
7854
7855     <row>
7856      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7857       <structfield>stxdinherit</structfield> <type>bool</type>
7858      </para>
7859      <para>
7860       If true, the stats include values from child tables, not just the
7861       values in the specified relation
7862      </para></entry>
7863     </row>
7864
7865     <row>
7866      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7867       <structfield>stxdndistinct</structfield> <type>pg_ndistinct</type>
7868      </para>
7869      <para>
7870       N-distinct counts, serialized as <structname>pg_ndistinct</structname> type
7871      </para></entry>
7872     </row>
7873
7874     <row>
7875      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7876       <structfield>stxddependencies</structfield> <type>pg_dependencies</type>
7877      </para>
7878      <para>
7879       Functional dependency statistics, serialized
7880       as <structname>pg_dependencies</structname> type
7881      </para></entry>
7882     </row>
7883
7884     <row>
7885      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7886       <structfield>stxdmcv</structfield> <type>pg_mcv_list</type>
7887      </para>
7888      <para>
7889       MCV (most-common values) list statistics, serialized as
7890       <structname>pg_mcv_list</structname> type
7891      </para></entry>
7892     </row>
7893
7894     <row>
7895      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7896       <structfield>stxdexpr</structfield> <type>pg_statistic[]</type>
7897      </para>
7898      <para>
7899       Per-expression statistics, serialized as an array of
7900       <structname>pg_statistic</structname> type
7901      </para></entry>
7902     </row>
7903    </tbody>
7904   </tgroup>
7905  </table>
7906
7907 </sect1>
7908
7909 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-subscription">
7910  <title><structname>pg_subscription</structname></title>
7911
7912  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-subscription">
7913   <primary>pg_subscription</primary>
7914  </indexterm>
7915
7916  <para>
7917   The catalog <structname>pg_subscription</structname> contains all existing
7918   logical replication subscriptions.  For more information about logical
7919   replication see <xref linkend="logical-replication"/>.
7920  </para>
7921
7922  <para>
7923   Unlike most system catalogs, <structname>pg_subscription</structname> is
7924   shared across all databases of a cluster: there is only one copy
7925   of <structname>pg_subscription</structname> per cluster, not one per
7926   database.
7927  </para>
7928
7929  <para>
7930   Access to the column <structfield>subconninfo</structfield> is revoked from
7931   normal users, because it could contain plain-text passwords.
7932  </para>
7933
7934  <table>
7935   <title><structname>pg_subscription</structname> Columns</title>
7936   <tgroup cols="1">
7937    <thead>
7938     <row>
7939      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7940       Column Type
7941      </para>
7942      <para>
7943       Description
7944      </para></entry>
7945     </row>
7946    </thead>
7947
7948    <tbody>
7949     <row>
7950      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7951       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7952      </para>
7953      <para>
7954       Row identifier
7955      </para></entry>
7956     </row>
7957
7958     <row>
7959      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7960       <structfield>subdbid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7961       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-database"><structname>pg_database</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7962      </para>
7963      <para>
7964       OID of the database that the subscription resides in
7965      </para></entry>
7966     </row>
7967
7968     <row>
7969      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7970       <structfield>subskiplsn</structfield> <type>pg_lsn</type>
7971      </para>
7972      <para>
7973       Finish LSN of the transaction whose changes are to be skipped, if a valid
7974       LSN; otherwise <literal>0/0</literal>.
7975      </para></entry>
7976     </row>
7977
7978     <row>
7979      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7980       <structfield>subname</structfield> <type>name</type>
7981      </para>
7982      <para>
7983       Name of the subscription
7984      </para></entry>
7985     </row>
7986
7987     <row>
7988      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7989       <structfield>subowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
7990       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
7991      </para>
7992      <para>
7993       Owner of the subscription
7994      </para></entry>
7995     </row>
7996
7997     <row>
7998      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
7999       <structfield>subenabled</structfield> <type>bool</type>
8000      </para>
8001      <para>
8002       If true, the subscription is enabled and should be replicating
8003      </para></entry>
8004     </row>
8005
8006    <row>
8007      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8008       <structfield>subbinary</structfield> <type>bool</type>
8009      </para>
8010      <para>
8011       If true, the subscription will request that the publisher send data
8012       in binary format
8013      </para></entry>
8014     </row>
8015
8016     <row>
8017      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8018       <structfield>substream</structfield> <type>char</type>
8019      </para>
8020      <para>
8021       Controls how to handle the streaming of in-progress transactions:
8022       <literal>f</literal> = disallow streaming of in-progress transactions,
8023       <literal>t</literal> = spill the changes of in-progress transactions to
8024       disk and apply at once after the transaction is committed on the
8025       publisher and received by the subscriber,
8026       <literal>p</literal> = apply changes directly using a parallel apply
8027       worker if available (same as <literal>t</literal> if no worker is
8028       available)
8029      </para></entry>
8030     </row>
8031
8032     <row>
8033      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8034       <structfield>subtwophasestate</structfield> <type>char</type>
8035      </para>
8036      <para>
8037       State codes for two-phase mode:
8038       <literal>d</literal> = disabled,
8039       <literal>p</literal> = pending enablement,
8040       <literal>e</literal> = enabled
8041      </para></entry>
8042     </row>
8043
8044     <row>
8045      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8046       <structfield>subdisableonerr</structfield> <type>bool</type>
8047      </para>
8048      <para>
8049       If true, the subscription will be disabled if one of its workers
8050       detects an error
8051      </para></entry>
8052     </row>
8053
8054     <row>
8055      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8056       <structfield>subpasswordrequired</structfield> <type>bool</type>
8057      </para>
8058      <para>
8059       If true, the subscription will be required to specify a password
8060       for authentication
8061      </para></entry>
8062     </row>
8063
8064     <row>
8065      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8066       <structfield>subrunasowner</structfield> <type>bool</type>
8067      </para>
8068      <para>
8069       If true, the subscription will be run with the permissions
8070       of the subscription owner
8071      </para></entry>
8072     </row>
8073
8074     <row>
8075      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8076       <structfield>subfailover</structfield> <type>bool</type>
8077      </para>
8078      <para>
8079       If true, the associated replication slots (i.e. the main slot and the
8080       table synchronization slots) in the upstream database are enabled to be
8081       synchronized to the standbys
8082      </para></entry>
8083     </row>
8084
8085     <row>
8086      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8087       <structfield>subconninfo</structfield> <type>text</type>
8088      </para>
8089      <para>
8090       Connection string to the upstream database
8091      </para></entry>
8092     </row>
8093
8094     <row>
8095      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8096       <structfield>subslotname</structfield> <type>name</type>
8097      </para>
8098      <para>
8099       Name of the replication slot in the upstream database (also used
8100       for the local replication origin name);
8101       null represents <literal>NONE</literal>
8102      </para></entry>
8103     </row>
8104
8105     <row>
8106      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8107       <structfield>subsynccommit</structfield> <type>text</type>
8108      </para>
8109      <para>
8110       The <varname>synchronous_commit</varname>
8111       setting for the subscription's workers to use
8112      </para></entry>
8113     </row>
8114
8115     <row>
8116      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8117       <structfield>subpublications</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
8118      </para>
8119      <para>
8120       Array of subscribed publication names. These reference
8121       publications defined in the upstream database. For more on publications
8122       see <xref linkend="logical-replication-publication"/>.
8123      </para></entry>
8124     </row>
8125
8126     <row>
8127      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8128       <structfield>suborigin</structfield> <type>text</type>
8129      </para>
8130      <para>
8131       The origin value must be either <literal>none</literal> or
8132       <literal>any</literal>. The default is <literal>any</literal>.
8133       If <literal>none</literal>, the subscription will request the publisher
8134       to only send changes that don't have an origin. If
8135       <literal>any</literal>, the publisher sends changes regardless of their
8136       origin.
8137      </para></entry>
8138     </row>
8139    </tbody>
8140   </tgroup>
8141  </table>
8142 </sect1>
8143
8144 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-subscription-rel">
8145  <title><structname>pg_subscription_rel</structname></title>
8146
8147  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-subscription-rel">
8148   <primary>pg_subscription_rel</primary>
8149  </indexterm>
8150
8151  <para>
8152   The catalog <structname>pg_subscription_rel</structname> contains the
8153   state for each replicated relation in each subscription.  This is a
8154   many-to-many mapping.
8155  </para>
8156
8157  <para>
8158   This catalog only contains tables known to the subscription after running
8159   either <link linkend="sql-createsubscription"><command>CREATE SUBSCRIPTION</command></link> or
8160   <link linkend="sql-altersubscription"><command>ALTER SUBSCRIPTION ... REFRESH
8161   PUBLICATION</command></link>.
8162  </para>
8163
8164  <table>
8165   <title><structname>pg_subscription_rel</structname> Columns</title>
8166   <tgroup cols="1">
8167    <thead>
8168     <row>
8169      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8170       Column Type
8171      </para>
8172      <para>
8173       Description
8174      </para></entry>
8175     </row>
8176    </thead>
8177
8178    <tbody>
8179     <row>
8180      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8181       <structfield>srsubid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8182       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-subscription"><structname>pg_subscription</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8183      </para>
8184      <para>
8185       Reference to subscription
8186      </para></entry>
8187     </row>
8188
8189     <row>
8190      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8191       <structfield>srrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8192       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8193      </para>
8194      <para>
8195       Reference to relation
8196      </para></entry>
8197     </row>
8198
8199     <row>
8200      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8201       <structfield>srsubstate</structfield> <type>char</type>
8202      </para>
8203      <para>
8204       State code:
8205       <literal>i</literal> = initialize,
8206       <literal>d</literal> = data is being copied,
8207       <literal>f</literal> = finished table copy,
8208       <literal>s</literal> = synchronized,
8209       <literal>r</literal> = ready (normal replication)
8210      </para></entry>
8211     </row>
8212
8213     <row>
8214      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8215       <structfield>srsublsn</structfield> <type>pg_lsn</type>
8216      </para>
8217      <para>
8218       Remote LSN of the state change used for synchronization coordination
8219       when in <literal>s</literal> or <literal>r</literal> states,
8220       otherwise null
8221      </para></entry>
8222     </row>
8223    </tbody>
8224   </tgroup>
8225  </table>
8226 </sect1>
8227
8228 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-tablespace">
8229  <title><structname>pg_tablespace</structname></title>
8230
8231  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-tablespace">
8232   <primary>pg_tablespace</primary>
8233  </indexterm>
8234
8235  <para>
8236   The catalog <structname>pg_tablespace</structname> stores information
8237   about the available tablespaces.  Tables can be placed in particular
8238   tablespaces to aid administration of disk layout.
8239  </para>
8240
8241  <para>
8242   Unlike most system catalogs, <structname>pg_tablespace</structname>
8243   is shared across all databases of a cluster: there is only one
8244   copy of <structname>pg_tablespace</structname> per cluster, not
8245   one per database.
8246  </para>
8247
8248  <table>
8249   <title><structname>pg_tablespace</structname> Columns</title>
8250   <tgroup cols="1">
8251    <thead>
8252     <row>
8253      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8254       Column Type
8255      </para>
8256      <para>
8257       Description
8258      </para></entry>
8259     </row>
8260    </thead>
8261
8262    <tbody>
8263     <row>
8264      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8265       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8266      </para>
8267      <para>
8268       Row identifier
8269      </para></entry>
8270     </row>
8271
8272     <row>
8273      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8274       <structfield>spcname</structfield> <type>name</type>
8275      </para>
8276      <para>
8277       Tablespace name
8278      </para></entry>
8279     </row>
8280
8281     <row>
8282      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8283       <structfield>spcowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8284       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8285      </para>
8286      <para>
8287       Owner of the tablespace, usually the user who created it
8288      </para></entry>
8289     </row>
8290
8291     <row>
8292      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8293       <structfield>spcacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
8294      </para>
8295      <para>
8296       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
8297      </para></entry>
8298     </row>
8299
8300     <row>
8301      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8302       <structfield>spcoptions</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
8303      </para>
8304      <para>
8305       Tablespace-level options, as <quote>keyword=value</quote> strings
8306      </para></entry>
8307     </row>
8308    </tbody>
8309   </tgroup>
8310  </table>
8311 </sect1>
8312
8313
8314 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-transform">
8315  <title><structname>pg_transform</structname></title>
8316
8317  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-transform">
8318   <primary>pg_transform</primary>
8319  </indexterm>
8320
8321  <para>
8322   The catalog <structname>pg_transform</structname> stores information about
8323   transforms, which are a mechanism to adapt data types to procedural
8324   languages.  See <xref linkend="sql-createtransform"/> for more information.
8325  </para>
8326
8327  <table>
8328   <title><structname>pg_transform</structname> Columns</title>
8329   <tgroup cols="1">
8330    <thead>
8331     <row>
8332      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8333       Column Type
8334      </para>
8335      <para>
8336       Description
8337      </para></entry>
8338     </row>
8339    </thead>
8340
8341    <tbody>
8342     <row>
8343      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8344       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8345      </para>
8346      <para>
8347       Row identifier
8348      </para></entry>
8349     </row>
8350
8351     <row>
8352      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8353       <structfield>trftype</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8354       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8355      </para>
8356      <para>
8357       OID of the data type this transform is for
8358      </para></entry>
8359     </row>
8360
8361     <row>
8362      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8363       <structfield>trflang</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8364       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-language"><structname>pg_language</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8365      </para>
8366      <para>
8367       OID of the language this transform is for
8368      </para></entry>
8369     </row>
8370
8371     <row>
8372      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8373       <structfield>trffromsql</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
8374       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8375      </para>
8376      <para>
8377       The OID of the function to use when converting the data type for input
8378       to the procedural language (e.g., function parameters).  Zero is stored
8379       if the default behavior should be used.
8380      </para></entry>
8381     </row>
8382
8383     <row>
8384      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8385       <structfield>trftosql</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
8386       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8387      </para>
8388      <para>
8389       The OID of the function to use when converting output from the
8390       procedural language (e.g., return values) to the data type.  Zero is
8391       stored if the default behavior should be used.
8392      </para></entry>
8393     </row>
8394    </tbody>
8395   </tgroup>
8396  </table>
8397 </sect1>
8398
8399
8400 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-trigger">
8401  <title><structname>pg_trigger</structname></title>
8402
8403  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-trigger">
8404   <primary>pg_trigger</primary>
8405  </indexterm>
8406
8407  <para>
8408   The catalog <structname>pg_trigger</structname> stores triggers on tables
8409   and views.
8410   See <xref linkend="sql-createtrigger"/>
8411   for more information.
8412  </para>
8413
8414  <table>
8415   <title><structname>pg_trigger</structname> Columns</title>
8416   <tgroup cols="1">
8417    <thead>
8418     <row>
8419      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8420       Column Type
8421      </para>
8422      <para>
8423       Description
8424      </para></entry>
8425     </row>
8426    </thead>
8427
8428    <tbody>
8429     <row>
8430      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8431       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8432      </para>
8433      <para>
8434       Row identifier
8435      </para></entry>
8436     </row>
8437
8438     <row>
8439      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8440       <structfield>tgrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8441       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8442      </para>
8443      <para>
8444       The table this trigger is on
8445      </para></entry>
8446     </row>
8447
8448     <row>
8449      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8450       <structfield>tgparentid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8451       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-trigger"><structname>pg_trigger</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8452      </para>
8453      <para>
8454       Parent trigger that this trigger is cloned from (this happens when
8455       partitions are created or attached to a partitioned table);
8456       zero if not a clone
8457      </para></entry>
8458     </row>
8459
8460     <row>
8461      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8462       <structfield>tgname</structfield> <type>name</type>
8463      </para>
8464      <para>
8465       Trigger name (must be unique among triggers of same table)
8466      </para></entry>
8467     </row>
8468
8469     <row>
8470      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8471       <structfield>tgfoid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8472       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8473      </para>
8474      <para>
8475       The function to be called
8476      </para></entry>
8477     </row>
8478
8479     <row>
8480      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8481       <structfield>tgtype</structfield> <type>int2</type>
8482      </para>
8483      <para>
8484       Bit mask identifying trigger firing conditions
8485      </para></entry>
8486     </row>
8487
8488     <row>
8489      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8490       <structfield>tgenabled</structfield> <type>char</type>
8491      </para>
8492      <para>
8493       Controls in which <xref linkend="guc-session-replication-role"/> modes
8494       the trigger fires.
8495       <literal>O</literal> = trigger fires in <quote>origin</quote> and <quote>local</quote> modes,
8496       <literal>D</literal> = trigger is disabled,
8497       <literal>R</literal> = trigger fires in <quote>replica</quote> mode,
8498       <literal>A</literal> = trigger fires always.
8499      </para></entry>
8500     </row>
8501
8502     <row>
8503      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8504       <structfield>tgisinternal</structfield> <type>bool</type>
8505      </para>
8506      <para>
8507       True if trigger is internally generated (usually, to enforce
8508       the constraint identified by <structfield>tgconstraint</structfield>)
8509      </para></entry>
8510     </row>
8511
8512     <row>
8513      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8514       <structfield>tgconstrrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8515       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8516      </para>
8517      <para>
8518       The table referenced by a referential integrity constraint
8519       (zero if trigger is not for a referential integrity constraint)
8520      </para></entry>
8521     </row>
8522
8523     <row>
8524      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8525       <structfield>tgconstrindid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8526       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8527      </para>
8528      <para>
8529       The index supporting a unique, primary key, referential integrity,
8530       or exclusion constraint
8531       (zero if trigger is not for one of these types of constraint)
8532      </para></entry>
8533     </row>
8534
8535     <row>
8536      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8537       <structfield>tgconstraint</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8538       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-constraint"><structname>pg_constraint</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8539      </para>
8540      <para>
8541       The <link linkend="catalog-pg-constraint"><structname>pg_constraint</structname></link> entry associated with the trigger
8542       (zero if trigger is not for a constraint)
8543      </para></entry>
8544     </row>
8545
8546     <row>
8547      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8548       <structfield>tgdeferrable</structfield> <type>bool</type>
8549      </para>
8550      <para>
8551       True if constraint trigger is deferrable
8552      </para></entry>
8553     </row>
8554
8555     <row>
8556      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8557       <structfield>tginitdeferred</structfield> <type>bool</type>
8558      </para>
8559      <para>
