8560       True if constraint trigger is initially deferred
8561      </para></entry>
8562     </row>
8563
8564     <row>
8565      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8566       <structfield>tgnargs</structfield> <type>int2</type>
8567      </para>
8568      <para>
8569       Number of argument strings passed to trigger function
8570      </para></entry>
8571     </row>
8572
8573     <row>
8574      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8575       <structfield>tgattr</structfield> <type>int2vector</type>
8576       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link>.<structfield>attnum</structfield>)
8577      </para>
8578      <para>
8579       Column numbers, if trigger is column-specific; otherwise an
8580       empty array
8581      </para></entry>
8582     </row>
8583
8584     <row>
8585      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8586       <structfield>tgargs</structfield> <type>bytea</type>
8587      </para>
8588      <para>
8589       Argument strings to pass to trigger, each NULL-terminated
8590      </para></entry>
8591     </row>
8592
8593     <row>
8594      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8595       <structfield>tgqual</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
8596      </para>
8597      <para>
8598       Expression tree (in <function>nodeToString()</function>
8599       representation) for the trigger's <literal>WHEN</literal> condition, or null
8600       if none
8601      </para></entry>
8602     </row>
8603
8604     <row>
8605      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8606       <structfield>tgoldtable</structfield> <type>name</type>
8607      </para>
8608      <para>
8609       <literal>REFERENCING</literal> clause name for <literal>OLD TABLE</literal>,
8610       or null if none
8611      </para></entry>
8612     </row>
8613
8614     <row>
8615      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8616       <structfield>tgnewtable</structfield> <type>name</type>
8617      </para>
8618      <para>
8619       <literal>REFERENCING</literal> clause name for <literal>NEW TABLE</literal>,
8620       or null if none
8621      </para></entry>
8622     </row>
8623    </tbody>
8624   </tgroup>
8625  </table>
8626
8627  <para>
8628   Currently, column-specific triggering is supported only for
8629   <literal>UPDATE</literal> events, and so <structfield>tgattr</structfield> is relevant
8630   only for that event type.  <structfield>tgtype</structfield> might
8631   contain bits for other event types as well, but those are presumed
8632   to be table-wide regardless of what is in <structfield>tgattr</structfield>.
8633  </para>
8634
8635  <note>
8636   <para>
8637    When <structfield>tgconstraint</structfield> is nonzero,
8638    <structfield>tgconstrrelid</structfield>, <structfield>tgconstrindid</structfield>,
8639    <structfield>tgdeferrable</structfield>, and <structfield>tginitdeferred</structfield> are
8640    largely redundant with the referenced <link linkend="catalog-pg-constraint"><structname>pg_constraint</structname></link> entry.
8641    However, it is possible for a non-deferrable trigger to be associated
8642    with a deferrable constraint: foreign key constraints can have some
8643    deferrable and some non-deferrable triggers.
8644   </para>
8645  </note>
8646
8647  <note>
8648   <para>
8649    <literal>pg_class.relhastriggers</literal>
8650    must be true if a relation has any triggers in this catalog.
8651   </para>
8652  </note>
8653
8654 </sect1>
8655
8656
8657 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-ts-config">
8658  <title><structname>pg_ts_config</structname></title>
8659
8660  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-ts-config">
8661   <primary>pg_ts_config</primary>
8662  </indexterm>
8663
8664  <para>
8665   The <structname>pg_ts_config</structname> catalog contains entries
8666   representing text search configurations.  A configuration specifies
8667   a particular text search parser and a list of dictionaries to use
8668   for each of the parser's output token types.  The parser is shown
8669   in the <structname>pg_ts_config</structname> entry, but the
8670   token-to-dictionary mapping is defined by subsidiary entries in <link
8671   linkend="catalog-pg-ts-config-map"><structname>pg_ts_config_map</structname></link>.
8672  </para>
8673
8674  <para>
8675   <productname>PostgreSQL</productname>'s text search features are
8676   described at length in <xref linkend="textsearch"/>.
8677  </para>
8678
8679  <table>
8680   <title><structname>pg_ts_config</structname> Columns</title>
8681   <tgroup cols="1">
8682    <thead>
8683     <row>
8684      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8685       Column Type
8686      </para>
8687      <para>
8688       Description
8689      </para></entry>
8690     </row>
8691    </thead>
8692
8693    <tbody>
8694     <row>
8695      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8696       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8697      </para>
8698      <para>
8699       Row identifier
8700      </para></entry>
8701     </row>
8702
8703     <row>
8704      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8705       <structfield>cfgname</structfield> <type>name</type>
8706      </para>
8707      <para>
8708       Text search configuration name
8709      </para></entry>
8710     </row>
8711
8712     <row>
8713      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8714       <structfield>cfgnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8715       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8716      </para>
8717      <para>
8718       The OID of the namespace that contains this configuration
8719      </para></entry>
8720     </row>
8721
8722     <row>
8723      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8724       <structfield>cfgowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8725       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8726      </para>
8727      <para>
8728       Owner of the configuration
8729      </para></entry>
8730     </row>
8731
8732     <row>
8733      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8734       <structfield>cfgparser</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8735       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-ts-parser"><structname>pg_ts_parser</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8736      </para>
8737      <para>
8738       The OID of the text search parser for this configuration
8739      </para></entry>
8740     </row>
8741    </tbody>
8742   </tgroup>
8743  </table>
8744 </sect1>
8745
8746
8747 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-ts-config-map">
8748  <title><structname>pg_ts_config_map</structname></title>
8749
8750  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-ts-config-map">
8751   <primary>pg_ts_config_map</primary>
8752  </indexterm>
8753
8754  <para>
8755   The <structname>pg_ts_config_map</structname> catalog contains entries
8756   showing which text search dictionaries should be consulted, and in
8757   what order, for each output token type of each text search configuration's
8758   parser.
8759  </para>
8760
8761  <para>
8762   <productname>PostgreSQL</productname>'s text search features are
8763   described at length in <xref linkend="textsearch"/>.
8764  </para>
8765
8766  <table>
8767   <title><structname>pg_ts_config_map</structname> Columns</title>
8768   <tgroup cols="1">
8769    <thead>
8770     <row>
8771      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8772       Column Type
8773      </para>
8774      <para>
8775       Description
8776      </para></entry>
8777     </row>
8778    </thead>
8779
8780    <tbody>
8781     <row>
8782      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8783       <structfield>mapcfg</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8784       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-ts-config"><structname>pg_ts_config</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8785      </para>
8786      <para>
8787       The OID of the <link linkend="catalog-pg-ts-config"><structname>pg_ts_config</structname></link> entry owning this map entry
8788      </para></entry>
8789     </row>
8790
8791     <row>
8792      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8793       <structfield>maptokentype</structfield> <type>int4</type>
8794      </para>
8795      <para>
8796       A token type emitted by the configuration's parser
8797      </para></entry>
8798     </row>
8799
8800     <row>
8801      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8802       <structfield>mapseqno</structfield> <type>int4</type>
8803      </para>
8804      <para>
8805       Order in which to consult this entry (lower
8806       <structfield>mapseqno</structfield>s first)
8807      </para></entry>
8808     </row>
8809
8810     <row>
8811      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8812       <structfield>mapdict</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8813       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-ts-dict"><structname>pg_ts_dict</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8814      </para>
8815      <para>
8816       The OID of the text search dictionary to consult
8817      </para></entry>
8818     </row>
8819    </tbody>
8820   </tgroup>
8821  </table>
8822 </sect1>
8823
8824
8825 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-ts-dict">
8826  <title><structname>pg_ts_dict</structname></title>
8827
8828  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-ts-dict">
8829   <primary>pg_ts_dict</primary>
8830  </indexterm>
8831
8832  <para>
8833   The <structname>pg_ts_dict</structname> catalog contains entries
8834   defining text search dictionaries.  A dictionary depends on a text
8835   search template, which specifies all the implementation functions
8836   needed; the dictionary itself provides values for the user-settable
8837   parameters supported by the template.  This division of labor allows
8838   dictionaries to be created by unprivileged users.  The parameters
8839   are specified by a text string <structfield>dictinitoption</structfield>,
8840   whose format and meaning vary depending on the template.
8841  </para>
8842
8843  <para>
8844   <productname>PostgreSQL</productname>'s text search features are
8845   described at length in <xref linkend="textsearch"/>.
8846  </para>
8847
8848  <table>
8849   <title><structname>pg_ts_dict</structname> Columns</title>
8850   <tgroup cols="1">
8851    <thead>
8852     <row>
8853      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8854       Column Type
8855      </para>
8856      <para>
8857       Description
8858      </para></entry>
8859     </row>
8860    </thead>
8861
8862    <tbody>
8863     <row>
8864      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8865       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8866      </para>
8867      <para>
8868       Row identifier
8869      </para></entry>
8870     </row>
8871
8872     <row>
8873      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8874       <structfield>dictname</structfield> <type>name</type>
8875      </para>
8876      <para>
8877       Text search dictionary name
8878      </para></entry>
8879     </row>
8880
8881     <row>
8882      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8883       <structfield>dictnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8884       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8885      </para>
8886      <para>
8887       The OID of the namespace that contains this dictionary
8888      </para></entry>
8889     </row>
8890
8891     <row>
8892      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8893       <structfield>dictowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8894       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8895      </para>
8896      <para>
8897       Owner of the dictionary
8898      </para></entry>
8899     </row>
8900
8901     <row>
8902      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8903       <structfield>dicttemplate</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8904       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-ts-template"><structname>pg_ts_template</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8905      </para>
8906      <para>
8907       The OID of the text search template for this dictionary
8908      </para></entry>
8909     </row>
8910
8911     <row>
8912      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8913       <structfield>dictinitoption</structfield> <type>text</type>
8914      </para>
8915      <para>
8916       Initialization option string for the template
8917      </para></entry>
8918     </row>
8919    </tbody>
8920   </tgroup>
8921  </table>
8922 </sect1>
8923
8924
8925 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-ts-parser">
8926  <title><structname>pg_ts_parser</structname></title>
8927
8928  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-ts-parser">
8929   <primary>pg_ts_parser</primary>
8930  </indexterm>
8931
8932  <para>
8933   The <structname>pg_ts_parser</structname> catalog contains entries
8934   defining text search parsers.  A parser is responsible for splitting
8935   input text into lexemes and assigning a token type to each lexeme.
8936   Since a parser must be implemented by C-language-level functions,
8937   creation of new parsers is restricted to database superusers.
8938  </para>
8939
8940  <para>
8941   <productname>PostgreSQL</productname>'s text search features are
8942   described at length in <xref linkend="textsearch"/>.
8943  </para>
8944
8945  <table>
8946   <title><structname>pg_ts_parser</structname> Columns</title>
8947   <tgroup cols="1">
8948    <thead>
8949     <row>
8950      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8951       Column Type
8952      </para>
8953      <para>
8954       Description
8955      </para></entry>
8956     </row>
8957    </thead>
8958
8959    <tbody>
8960     <row>
8961      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8962       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8963      </para>
8964      <para>
8965       Row identifier
8966      </para></entry>
8967     </row>
8968
8969     <row>
8970      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8971       <structfield>prsname</structfield> <type>name</type>
8972      </para>
8973      <para>
8974       Text search parser name
8975      </para></entry>
8976     </row>
8977
8978     <row>
8979      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8980       <structfield>prsnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
8981       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8982      </para>
8983      <para>
8984       The OID of the namespace that contains this parser
8985      </para></entry>
8986     </row>
8987
8988     <row>
8989      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
8990       <structfield>prsstart</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
8991       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
8992      </para>
8993      <para>
8994       OID of the parser's startup function
8995      </para></entry>
8996     </row>
8997
8998     <row>
8999      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9000       <structfield>prstoken</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9001       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9002      </para>
9003      <para>
9004       OID of the parser's next-token function
9005      </para></entry>
9006     </row>
9007
9008     <row>
9009      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9010       <structfield>prsend</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9011       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9012      </para>
9013      <para>
9014       OID of the parser's shutdown function
9015      </para></entry>
9016     </row>
9017
9018     <row>
9019      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9020       <structfield>prsheadline</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9021       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9022      </para>
9023      <para>
9024       OID of the parser's headline function (zero if none)
9025      </para></entry>
9026     </row>
9027
9028     <row>
9029      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9030       <structfield>prslextype</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9031       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9032      </para>
9033      <para>
9034       OID of the parser's lextype function
9035      </para></entry>
9036     </row>
9037    </tbody>
9038   </tgroup>
9039  </table>
9040 </sect1>
9041
9042
9043 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-ts-template">
9044  <title><structname>pg_ts_template</structname></title>
9045
9046  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-ts-template">
9047   <primary>pg_ts_template</primary>
9048  </indexterm>
9049
9050  <para>
9051   The <structname>pg_ts_template</structname> catalog contains entries
9052   defining text search templates.  A template is the implementation
9053   skeleton for a class of text search dictionaries.
9054   Since a template must be implemented by C-language-level functions,
9055   creation of new templates is restricted to database superusers.
9056  </para>
9057
9058  <para>
9059   <productname>PostgreSQL</productname>'s text search features are
9060   described at length in <xref linkend="textsearch"/>.
9061  </para>
9062
9063  <table>
9064   <title><structname>pg_ts_template</structname> Columns</title>
9065   <tgroup cols="1">
9066    <thead>
9067     <row>
9068      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9069       Column Type
9070      </para>
9071      <para>
9072       Description
9073      </para></entry>
9074     </row>
9075    </thead>
9076
9077    <tbody>
9078     <row>
9079      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9080       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9081      </para>
9082      <para>
9083       Row identifier
9084      </para></entry>
9085     </row>
9086
9087     <row>
9088      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9089       <structfield>tmplname</structfield> <type>name</type>
9090      </para>
9091      <para>
9092       Text search template name
9093      </para></entry>
9094     </row>
9095
9096     <row>
9097      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9098       <structfield>tmplnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9099       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9100      </para>
9101      <para>
9102       The OID of the namespace that contains this template
9103      </para></entry>
9104     </row>
9105
9106     <row>
9107      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9108       <structfield>tmplinit</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9109       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9110      </para>
9111      <para>
9112       OID of the template's initialization function (zero if none)
9113      </para></entry>
9114     </row>
9115
9116     <row>
9117      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9118       <structfield>tmpllexize</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9119       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9120      </para>
9121      <para>
9122       OID of the template's lexize function
9123      </para></entry>
9124     </row>
9125    </tbody>
9126   </tgroup>
9127  </table>
9128 </sect1>
9129
9130
9131 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-type">
9132  <title><structname>pg_type</structname></title>
9133
9134  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-type">
9135   <primary>pg_type</primary>
9136  </indexterm>
9137
9138  <para>
9139   The catalog <structname>pg_type</structname> stores information about data
9140   types.  Base types and enum types (scalar types) are created with
9141   <link linkend="sql-createtype"><command>CREATE TYPE</command></link>, and
9142   domains with
9143   <link linkend="sql-createdomain"><command>CREATE DOMAIN</command></link>.
9144   A composite type is automatically created for each table in the database, to
9145   represent the row structure of the table.  It is also possible to create
9146   composite types with <command>CREATE TYPE AS</command>.
9147  </para>
9148
9149  <table>
9150   <title><structname>pg_type</structname> Columns</title>
9151   <tgroup cols="1">
9152    <thead>
9153     <row>
9154      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9155       Column Type
9156      </para>
9157      <para>
9158       Description
9159      </para></entry>
9160     </row>
9161    </thead>
9162
9163    <tbody>
9164     <row>
9165      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9166       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9167      </para>
9168      <para>
9169       Row identifier
9170      </para></entry>
9171     </row>
9172
9173     <row>
9174      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9175       <structfield>typname</structfield> <type>name</type>
9176      </para>
9177      <para>
9178       Data type name
9179      </para></entry>
9180     </row>
9181
9182     <row>
9183      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9184       <structfield>typnamespace</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9185       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-namespace"><structname>pg_namespace</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9186      </para>
9187      <para>
9188       The OID of the namespace that contains this type
9189      </para></entry>
9190     </row>
9191
9192     <row>
9193      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9194       <structfield>typowner</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9195       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9196      </para>
9197      <para>
9198       Owner of the type
9199      </para></entry>
9200     </row>
9201
9202     <row>
9203      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9204       <structfield>typlen</structfield> <type>int2</type>
9205      </para>
9206      <para>
9207       For a fixed-size type, <structfield>typlen</structfield> is the number
9208       of bytes in the internal representation of the type.  But for a
9209       variable-length type, <structfield>typlen</structfield> is negative.
9210       -1 indicates a <quote>varlena</quote> type (one that has a length word),
9211       -2 indicates a null-terminated C string.
9212      </para></entry>
9213     </row>
9214
9215     <row>
9216      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9217       <structfield>typbyval</structfield> <type>bool</type>
9218      </para>
9219      <para>
9220       <structfield>typbyval</structfield> determines whether internal
9221       routines pass a value of this type by value or by reference.
9222       <structfield>typbyval</structfield> had better be false if
9223       <structfield>typlen</structfield> is not 1, 2, or 4 (or 8 on machines
9224       where Datum is 8 bytes).
9225       Variable-length types are always passed by reference. Note that
9226       <structfield>typbyval</structfield> can be false even if the
9227       length would allow pass-by-value.
9228      </para></entry>
9229     </row>
9230
9231     <row>
9232      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9233       <structfield>typtype</structfield> <type>char</type>
9234      </para>
9235      <para>
9236       <structfield>typtype</structfield> is
9237       <literal>b</literal> for a base type,
9238       <literal>c</literal> for a composite type (e.g., a table's row type),
9239       <literal>d</literal> for a domain,
9240       <literal>e</literal> for an enum type,
9241       <literal>p</literal> for a pseudo-type,
9242       <literal>r</literal> for a range type, or
9243       <literal>m</literal> for a multirange type.
9244       See also <structfield>typrelid</structfield> and
9245       <structfield>typbasetype</structfield>.
9246      </para></entry>
9247     </row>
9248
9249     <row>
9250      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9251       <structfield>typcategory</structfield> <type>char</type>
9252      </para>
9253      <para>
9254       <structfield>typcategory</structfield> is an arbitrary classification
9255       of data types that is used by the parser to determine which implicit
9256       casts should be <quote>preferred</quote>.
9257       See <xref linkend="catalog-typcategory-table"/>.
9258      </para></entry>
9259     </row>
9260
9261     <row>
9262      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9263       <structfield>typispreferred</structfield> <type>bool</type>
9264      </para>
9265      <para>
9266       True if the type is a preferred cast target within its
9267       <structfield>typcategory</structfield>
9268      </para></entry>
9269     </row>
9270
9271     <row>
9272      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9273       <structfield>typisdefined</structfield> <type>bool</type>
9274      </para>
9275      <para>
9276       True if the type is defined, false if this is a placeholder
9277       entry for a not-yet-defined type.  When
9278       <structfield>typisdefined</structfield> is false, nothing
9279       except the type name, namespace, and OID can be relied on.
9280      </para></entry>
9281     </row>
9282
9283     <row>
9284      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9285       <structfield>typdelim</structfield> <type>char</type>
9286      </para>
9287      <para>
9288       Character that separates two values of this type when parsing
9289       array input.  Note that the delimiter is associated with the array
9290       element data type, not the array data type.
9291      </para></entry>
9292     </row>
9293
9294     <row>
9295      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9296       <structfield>typrelid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9297       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9298      </para>
9299      <para>
9300       If this is a composite type (see
9301       <structfield>typtype</structfield>), then this column points to
9302       the <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link> entry that defines the
9303       corresponding table.  (For a free-standing composite type, the
9304       <link linkend="catalog-pg-class"><structname>pg_class</structname></link> entry doesn't really represent
9305       a table, but it is needed anyway for the type's
9306       <link linkend="catalog-pg-attribute"><structname>pg_attribute</structname></link> entries to link to.)
9307       Zero for non-composite types.
9308      </para></entry>
9309     </row>
9310
9311     <row>
9312      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9313       <structfield>typsubscript</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9314       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9315      </para>
9316      <para>
9317       Subscripting handler function's OID, or zero if this type doesn't
9318       support subscripting.  Types that are <quote>true</quote> array
9319       types have <structfield>typsubscript</structfield>
9320       = <function>array_subscript_handler</function>, but other types may
9321       have other handler functions to implement specialized subscripting
9322       behavior.
9323      </para></entry>
9324     </row>
9325
9326     <row>
9327      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9328       <structfield>typelem</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9329       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9330      </para>
9331      <para>
9332       If <structfield>typelem</structfield> is not zero then it
9333       identifies another row in <structname>pg_type</structname>,
9334       defining the type yielded by subscripting.  This should be zero
9335       if <structfield>typsubscript</structfield> is zero.  However, it can
9336       be zero when <structfield>typsubscript</structfield> isn't zero, if the
9337       handler doesn't need <structfield>typelem</structfield> to
9338       determine the subscripting result type.
9339       Note that a <structfield>typelem</structfield> dependency is
9340       considered to imply physical containment of the element type in
9341       this type; so DDL changes on the element type might be restricted
9342       by the presence of this type.
9343      </para></entry>
9344     </row>
9345
9346     <row>
9347      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9348       <structfield>typarray</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9349       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9350      </para>
9351      <para>
9352       If <structfield>typarray</structfield> is not zero then it
9353       identifies another row in <structname>pg_type</structname>, which
9354       is the <quote>true</quote> array type having this type as element
9355      </para></entry>
9356     </row>
9357
9358     <row>
9359      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9360       <structfield>typinput</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9361       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9362      </para>
9363      <para>
9364       Input conversion function (text format)
9365      </para></entry>
9366     </row>
9367
9368     <row>
9369      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9370       <structfield>typoutput</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9371       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9372      </para>
9373      <para>
9374       Output conversion function (text format)
9375      </para></entry>
9376     </row>
9377
9378     <row>
9379      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9380       <structfield>typreceive</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9381       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9382      </para>
9383      <para>
9384       Input conversion function (binary format), or zero if none
9385      </para></entry>
9386     </row>
9387
9388     <row>
9389      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9390       <structfield>typsend</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9391       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9392      </para>
9393      <para>
9394       Output conversion function (binary format), or zero if none
9395      </para></entry>
9396     </row>
9397
9398     <row>
9399      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9400       <structfield>typmodin</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9401       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9402      </para>
9403      <para>
9404       Type modifier input function, or zero if type does not support modifiers
9405      </para></entry>
9406     </row>
9407
9408     <row>
9409      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9410       <structfield>typmodout</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9411       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9412      </para>
9413      <para>
9414       Type modifier output function, or zero to use the standard format
9415      </para></entry>
9416     </row>
9417
9418     <row>
9419      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9420       <structfield>typanalyze</structfield> <type>regproc</type>
9421       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-proc"><structname>pg_proc</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9422      </para>
9423      <para>
9424       Custom <xref linkend="sql-analyze"/> function,
9425       or zero to use the standard function
9426      </para></entry>
9427     </row>
9428
9429     <row>
9430      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9431       <structfield>typalign</structfield> <type>char</type>
9432      </para>
9433      <para>
9434       <structfield>typalign</structfield> is the alignment required
9435       when storing a value of this type.  It applies to storage on
9436       disk as well as most representations of the value inside
9437       <productname>PostgreSQL</productname>.
9438       When multiple values are stored consecutively, such
9439       as in the representation of a complete row on disk, padding is
9440       inserted before a datum of this type so that it begins on the
9441       specified boundary.  The alignment reference is the beginning
9442       of the first datum in the sequence.
9443       Possible values are:
9444       <itemizedlist>
9445        <listitem>
9446         <para><literal>c</literal> = <type>char</type> alignment, i.e., no alignment needed.</para>
9447        </listitem>
9448        <listitem>
9449         <para><literal>s</literal> = <type>short</type> alignment (2 bytes on most machines).</para>
9450        </listitem>
9451        <listitem>
9452         <para><literal>i</literal> = <type>int</type> alignment (4 bytes on most machines).</para>
9453        </listitem>
9454        <listitem>
9455         <para><literal>d</literal> = <type>double</type> alignment (8 bytes on many machines, but by no means all).</para>
9456        </listitem>
9457       </itemizedlist>
9458      </para></entry>
9459     </row>
9460
9461     <row>
9462      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9463       <structfield>typstorage</structfield> <type>char</type>
9464      </para>
9465      <para>
9466       <structfield>typstorage</structfield> tells for varlena
9467       types (those with <structfield>typlen</structfield> = -1) if
9468       the type is prepared for toasting and what the default strategy
9469       for attributes of this type should be.
9470       Possible values are:
9471       <itemizedlist>
9472        <listitem>
9473         <para>
9474          <literal>p</literal> (plain): Values must always be stored plain
9475          (non-varlena types always use this value).
9476         </para>
9477        </listitem>
9478        <listitem>
9479         <para>
9480          <literal>e</literal> (external): Values can be stored in a
9481          secondary <quote>TOAST</quote> relation (if relation has one, see
9482          <literal>pg_class.reltoastrelid</literal>).
9483         </para>
9484        </listitem>
9485        <listitem>
9486         <para>
9487          <literal>m</literal> (main): Values can be compressed and stored
9488          inline.
9489         </para>
9490        </listitem>
9491        <listitem>
9492         <para>
9493          <literal>x</literal> (extended): Values can be compressed and/or
9494          moved to a secondary relation.
9495         </para>
9496        </listitem>
9497       </itemizedlist>
9498       <literal>x</literal> is the usual choice for toast-able types.
9499       Note that <literal>m</literal> values can also be moved out to
9500       secondary storage, but only as a last resort (<literal>e</literal>
9501       and <literal>x</literal> values are moved first).
9502      </para></entry>
9503     </row>
9504
9505     <row>
9506      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9507       <structfield>typnotnull</structfield> <type>bool</type>
9508      </para>
9509      <para>
9510       <structfield>typnotnull</structfield> represents a not-null
9511       constraint on a type.  Used for domains only.
9512      </para></entry>
9513     </row>
9514
9515     <row>
9516      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9517       <structfield>typbasetype</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9518       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-type"><structname>pg_type</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9519      </para>
9520      <para>
9521       If this is a domain (see <structfield>typtype</structfield>), then
9522       <structfield>typbasetype</structfield> identifies the type that this
9523       one is based on.  Zero if this type is not a domain.
9524      </para></entry>
9525     </row>
9526
9527     <row>
9528      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9529       <structfield>typtypmod</structfield> <type>int4</type>
9530      </para>
9531      <para>
9532       Domains use <structfield>typtypmod</structfield> to record the <literal>typmod</literal>
9533       to be applied to their base type (-1 if base type does not use a
9534       <literal>typmod</literal>).  -1 if this type is not a domain.
9535      </para></entry>
9536     </row>
9537
9538     <row>
9539      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9540       <structfield>typndims</structfield> <type>int4</type>
9541      </para>
9542      <para>
9543       <structfield>typndims</structfield> is the number of array dimensions
9544       for a domain over an array (that is, <structfield>typbasetype</structfield> is
9545       an array type).
9546       Zero for types other than domains over array types.
9547      </para></entry>
9548     </row>
9549
9550     <row>
9551      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9552       <structfield>typcollation</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9553       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-collation"><structname>pg_collation</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9554      </para>
9555      <para>
9556       <structfield>typcollation</structfield> specifies the collation
9557       of the type.  If the type does not support collations, this will
9558       be zero.  A base type that supports collations will have a nonzero
9559       value here, typically <symbol>DEFAULT_COLLATION_OID</symbol>.
9560       A domain over a collatable type can have a collation OID different
9561       from its base type's, if one was specified for the domain.
9562      </para></entry>
9563     </row>
9564
9565     <row>
9566      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9567       <structfield>typdefaultbin</structfield> <type>pg_node_tree</type>
9568      </para>
9569      <para>
9570       If <structfield>typdefaultbin</structfield> is not null, it is the
9571       <function>nodeToString()</function>
9572       representation of a default expression for the type.  This is
9573       only used for domains.
9574      </para></entry>
9575     </row>
9576
9577     <row>
9578      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9579       <structfield>typdefault</structfield> <type>text</type>
9580      </para>
9581      <para>
9582       <structfield>typdefault</structfield> is null if the type has no associated
9583       default value. If <structfield>typdefaultbin</structfield> is not null,
9584       <structfield>typdefault</structfield> must contain a human-readable version of the
9585       default expression represented by <structfield>typdefaultbin</structfield>.  If
9586       <structfield>typdefaultbin</structfield> is null and <structfield>typdefault</structfield> is
9587       not, then <structfield>typdefault</structfield> is the external representation of
9588       the type's default value, which can be fed to the type's input
9589       converter to produce a constant.
9590      </para></entry>
9591     </row>
9592
9593     <row>
9594      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9595       <structfield>typacl</structfield> <type>aclitem[]</type>
9596      </para>
9597      <para>
9598       Access privileges; see <xref linkend="ddl-priv"/> for details
9599      </para></entry>
9600     </row>
9601    </tbody>
9602   </tgroup>
9603  </table>
9604
9605  <note>
9606   <para>
9607    For fixed-width types used in system tables, it is critical that the size
9608    and alignment defined in <structname>pg_type</structname>
9609    agree with the way that the compiler will lay out the column in
9610    a structure representing a table row.
9611   </para>
9612  </note>
9613
9614  <para>
9615   <xref linkend="catalog-typcategory-table"/> lists the system-defined values
9616   of <structfield>typcategory</structfield>.  Any future additions to this list will
9617   also be upper-case ASCII letters.  All other ASCII characters are reserved
9618   for user-defined categories.
9619  </para>
9620
9621  <table id="catalog-typcategory-table">
9622   <title><structfield>typcategory</structfield> Codes</title>
9623
9624   <tgroup cols="2">
9625    <thead>
9626     <row>
9627      <entry>Code</entry>
9628      <entry>Category</entry>
9629     </row>
9630    </thead>
9631
9632    <tbody>
9633     <row>
9634      <entry><literal>A</literal></entry>
9635      <entry>Array types</entry>
9636     </row>
9637     <row>
9638      <entry><literal>B</literal></entry>
9639      <entry>Boolean types</entry>
9640     </row>
9641     <row>
9642      <entry><literal>C</literal></entry>
9643      <entry>Composite types</entry>
9644     </row>
9645     <row>
9646      <entry><literal>D</literal></entry>
9647      <entry>Date/time types</entry>
9648     </row>
9649     <row>
9650      <entry><literal>E</literal></entry>
9651      <entry>Enum types</entry>
9652     </row>
9653     <row>
9654      <entry><literal>G</literal></entry>
9655      <entry>Geometric types</entry>
9656     </row>
9657     <row>
9658      <entry><literal>I</literal></entry>
9659      <entry>Network address types</entry>
9660     </row>
9661     <row>
9662      <entry><literal>N</literal></entry>
9663      <entry>Numeric types</entry>
9664     </row>
9665     <row>
9666      <entry><literal>P</literal></entry>
9667      <entry>Pseudo-types</entry>
9668     </row>
9669     <row>
9670      <entry><literal>R</literal></entry>
9671      <entry>Range types</entry>
9672     </row>
9673     <row>
9674      <entry><literal>S</literal></entry>
9675      <entry>String types</entry>
9676     </row>
9677     <row>
9678      <entry><literal>T</literal></entry>
9679      <entry>Timespan types</entry>
9680     </row>
9681     <row>
9682      <entry><literal>U</literal></entry>
9683      <entry>User-defined types</entry>
9684     </row>
9685     <row>
9686      <entry><literal>V</literal></entry>
9687      <entry>Bit-string types</entry>
9688     </row>
9689     <row>
9690      <entry><literal>X</literal></entry>
9691      <entry><type>unknown</type> type</entry>
9692     </row>
9693     <row>
9694      <entry><literal>Z</literal></entry>
9695      <entry>Internal-use types</entry>
9696     </row>
9697    </tbody>
9698   </tgroup>
9699  </table>
9700
9701 </sect1>
9702
9703
9704 <sect1 id="catalog-pg-user-mapping">
9705  <title><structname>pg_user_mapping</structname></title>
9706
9707  <indexterm zone="catalog-pg-user-mapping">
9708   <primary>pg_user_mapping</primary>
9709  </indexterm>
9710
9711  <para>
9712   The catalog <structname>pg_user_mapping</structname> stores
9713   the mappings from local user to remote.  Access to this catalog is
9714   restricted from normal users, use the view
9715   <link linkend="view-pg-user-mappings"><structname>pg_user_mappings</structname></link>
9716   instead.
9717  </para>
9718
9719  <table>
9720   <title><structname>pg_user_mapping</structname> Columns</title>
9721   <tgroup cols="1">
9722    <thead>
9723     <row>
9724      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9725       Column Type
9726      </para>
9727      <para>
9728       Description
9729      </para></entry>
9730     </row>
9731    </thead>
9732
9733    <tbody>
9734     <row>
9735      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9736       <structfield>oid</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9737      </para>
9738      <para>
9739       Row identifier
9740      </para></entry>
9741     </row>
9742
9743     <row>
9744      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9745       <structfield>umuser</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9746       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-authid"><structname>pg_authid</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9747      </para>
9748      <para>
9749       OID of the local role being mapped, or zero if the user mapping is public
9750      </para></entry>
9751     </row>
9752
9753     <row>
9754      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9755       <structfield>umserver</structfield> <type>oid</type>
9756       (references <link linkend="catalog-pg-foreign-server"><structname>pg_foreign_server</structname></link>.<structfield>oid</structfield>)
9757      </para>
9758      <para>
9759       The OID of the foreign server that contains this mapping
9760      </para></entry>
9761     </row>
9762
9763     <row>
9764      <entry role="catalog_table_entry"><para role="column_definition">
9765       <structfield>umoptions</structfield> <type>text[]</type>
9766      </para>
9767      <para>
9768       User mapping specific options, as <quote>keyword=value</quote> strings
9769      </para></entry>
9770     </row>
9771    </tbody>
9772   </tgroup>
9773  </table>
9774 </sect1>
9775
9776</chapter>
